title: [ONLINE] R-Ladies RTP Informal Hangout & Networking
description:
Please join us for an informal hangout and networking hour with other R-Ladies RTP members.

**Topic:** At this meetup, we'll informally talk about what we're working on in R, what we've learned recently, and discuss what we'd like to learn in 2024. This includes discussing events that we'd like to see hosted by R-Ladies RTP for 2024. Come and join the discussion and share your learning goals and wishes for the new year.

**Format:** This will be an informal hour-long Zoom meeting with breakout rooms to discuss a set of discussion questions in small groups. We will also leave time to have unstructured conversation about our experiences programming in R.

**Pre-requisites:** None. This meeting is intended for all R-Ladies RTP members, regardless of your R skill level.

**Code of Conduct:** All events are intended for women and nonbinary people as leaders, mentors, members, and attendees. We are emphatically queer and trans friendly. Men are welcomed to attend *as guests* and must be accompanied by the R-Lady who invited them. This group was created so those who identify as women or nonbinary have a comfortable place to learn. If you would like to attend and feel excluded, please introduce yourself to an organizer.

**Contact:** If you have any questions or concerns, please reach out to organizer, Sheila Saia, on meetup.

title: Delavnica: PONOVLJIVA ZNANOST? (reproducible research)
description:
Na tokratni delavnici bomo poskusili:
\- potegnili kodo iz githuba\,
\- ponovili analizo \(narisali graf ali dva\)
\- primerjali rezultate med seboj\!

Vabljeni vsi, ki vas zanimajo orodja za ponovljivo znanost in R!

On this workshop we will :
\- pull code and data from github
\- reproduce 1 or 2 figures made with the data and code
\- compare results\!

Welcome all who are interested!

Geslo ZOOM: RLadies

title: Data Visualization Practice Session - in Italian
description:
**🎬 It's time to put things into practice. 🔥**

🇮🇹 Dopo due sessioni fantastiche con esperti di data visualization, è giunto il momento di mettere in pratica quanto appreso!

Vi invitiamo a partecipare a una sessione pratica di visualizzazione dati, dove avremo l'opportunità di applicare le conoscenze acquisite con le guide di professionisti del settore. Sarà un'occasione per affinare le nostre abilità, porre domande e condividere esperienze.

Non perdete l'opportunità di crescere insieme come comunità! Vi aspettiamo numerosi per questa esperienza formativa.

Perparati con tutto il necessario:

1. Login to [https://posit.cloud/](https://posit.cloud/)
2. Crea un nuovo Project chiamato: **DataViz-Practice**
3. Installa i pacchetti necessari:
pkgs <- c("tidyverse","ggthemes","sysfonts","showtext","cowplot","ggtext")
install.packages(pkgs)

title: De Hojas de cálculo a Scripts: Razones para adoptar R sobre Excel
description:
Hola R-Ladies y Aliados
Estamos muy emocionadas de volvernos a encontrar en nuestro evento de cierre de año, donde contaremos con la participación de una destacada R-Lady: Sherly Tarazona.
Sherly estudió Ingeniería Estadística en la Universidad Nacional de Ingeniería, llevó una Especialización en Business Intelligence and Business Analytics, cuenta con una Maestría en Análisis y Visualización en Datos Masivos y actualmente se desempeña como Jefa de Advanced Analytics en Caja Arequipa y como Co-Organizadora de la comunidad R-Ladies Lima.
Entre sus hobbies está jugar voley, viajar, participar en proyectos con impacto social y le encantan los gatitos, ella es Rcatlover ^^

**• Información del tema:**
Hoy en día una habilidad muy apreciada en el campo de Data & Analytics es saber programar. Excel es una buena herramienta, pero los skills de estos profesionales puede verse reforzados si aprende a usar un lenguaje de programación para el análisis de datos como R. En esta sesión, se mostrará las razones para pasar de hojas de cálculo a crear script de R.

**• Requisitos:**
\- Asegúrate de tener instalado Zoom\.
\- Tener instalado R y RStudio \(de preferencia la versión más actualizada\)\.

**• Zoom:**
**[https://us02web.zoom.us/j/86327099333?pwd=VVY4eEJNNDIvaEpWVFU2RkZFUDk0UT09](https://us02web.zoom.us/j/86327099333?pwd=VVY4eEJNNDIvaEpWVFU2RkZFUDk0UT09)**
**Clave:** 576771

***IMPORTANTE***
Para participar, dale "Asistiré" en este evento.
Por razones de seguridad no compartiremos el link para ingresar a Zoom de manera pública. Días antes del evento estaremos enviando un correo con el link de zoom a sus correos de meetup.

**• Pregunta frecuente:**
P: ¿Los chicos son bienvenidos a las reuniones de R-Ladies Lima?
R: Sí, los chicos son bienvenidos a cualquiera de nuestros eventos. Puedes revisar nuestro código de conducta global aquí: https://github.com/rladies/starter-kit/wiki/Code-of-Conduct

title: Introduction to reproducible reporting with Quarto
description:
**Join us for a fun workshop that will introduce you to Quarto - an exciting tool for creating reports and websites**

Quarto is an open-source scientific and technical publishing system, developed by Posit (formerly RStudio). By combining narrative text, code, images, and tables, Quarto allows you to produce elegantly formatted output that is fully reproducible. Quarto is capable of embedding code written in R, Python, Julia, and Observable JS (for this talk we’ll focus on R). It supports dozens of static and dynamic output formats including HTML, PDF, MS Word, slides, dashboards, scientific articles and more.
In this talk, we’ll discuss what Quarto is and how it differs from R Markdown, demonstrate how to make simple documents, and showcase a few features of Quarto that make it incredibly useful. In this session, you will learn how to:

* create documents in Quarto that combine R code with text, images, and plots.
* make use of Quarto options to easily switch between output formats such as PDF, MS Word, and HTML
* parameterise reports to quickly create multiple copies of a report

\*\*Please bring a laptop so you can fully participate in the workshop.\*\*

After the workshop, we will have ample time for networking and chat over some snacks and drinks (courtesy of the Software Sustainability Institute).

*Nicola is a Lecturer in Health Data Science, based at Lancaster University.*

A gente brilha TOGETHER!

title: Advent of Code with R-Ladies Philly
description:
[Advent of Code](https://adventofcode.com/) is an annual programming challenge where participants take on (increasingly difficult) puzzles each day from December 1st to 25th.

While you can use **any** programming language, many people challenge themselves to complete the puzzles in R - and you absolutely can solve them all in R!

This is a beginner-friendly event to look at the first 7 days of advent of code and solve some puzzles together! If you are curious about this type of programming challenge, but need that extra push to check it out, then this is a perfect event to attend. We will do a gentle introduction to advent of code and solve a few puzzles together.

This is an online event - full Zoom details will be shared closer to the event.

title: RLadies Santa Barbra 2023 Wrap-up!
description:
Dear Community,

Join us for our 2023 wrap-up event on **Wednesday, December 6, at Institution Ale**., Downtown Santa Barbara. We will meet from **5:30 to 7:00 pm and do a #TidyTuesday style activity.**

[TidyTuesday](https://github.com/rfordatascience/tidytuesday/tree/master) is a weekly social data project organized by the [R4DS ](https://rfordatasci.com/)community. Every Monday, R4DS posts a different real-world data set. The idea is to use this data set to explore and learn new things about working with and visualizing data.

We invite you all to bring your computers, grab a drink and/or snack, and have fun diving into the data set of the week (or any data you’d like). As we code together, we can help and learn from one another.

**Everyone is welcome to join, whether you’re a veteran Meetup attendee or new to R and the Santa Barbara community!** We strive to create a safe and inclusive environment for all R-Ladies events and ask all interested participants to review our [code of conduct](https://guide.rladies.org/about/coc/) before attending.

See you there,
Camila, Danielle & Sam

Ps. We will share the TidyTuesday data of that week here once it is announced.

title: Ten tips for better text: typography meets ggplot and friends
description:
**Join us** for an engaging talk on **transforming data stories** through effective text management, color usage, and dynamic content.

Data Visualization Expert **Cara Thompson** will provide valuable insights and practical coding tips to enhance your data visualizations in R. **Don't miss this opportunity to elevate your data storytelling skills!**

There are often many facets to our data stories, which we need to present succinctly enough for our readers to want to engage with.

In this talk, we will explore how to make text work for us, by first considering how much of it we really need.

Once we've decluttered and explored how we can use colours to be less text-dependent, we'll look at how to optimise text hierarchy in descriptions and in-plot annotations to keep the main thing the main thing, and how to create dynamic content and alignments.

Finally, we'll explore coding tricks to apply these typography tips to tables and interactive plots, giving readers additional information on demand.

Throughout the talk, Cara will share the packages and code snippets used to create and modify plots in R straight from readily available data.

title: Introduction to ChatGPT for R coding
description:
Join us as we welcome Olivia Weng from Washington University in St. Louis for an introduction to using ChatGPT to assist you with your R coding. Learn how to use ChatGPT, step-by-step, to generate R code for preliminary data cleaning and data visualization. This presentation covers data import, descriptive statistics, data cleaning with dplyr, and data visualization with ggplot2 with writing effective ChatGPT prompts.

**You can join in to just watch and learn about this resource, or, if you want to follow along in R and ChatGPT with Olivia, you will need to have installed [R](https://cran.r-project.org/) and [R Studio Desktop](https://posit.co/download/rstudio-desktop/) and have a free [OpenAI ChatGPT account](https://chat.openai.com/auth/login).**

To follow along, you also need to download the two datasets that Olivia will use:

* [https://www.kaggle.com/datasets/prasertk/healthy-lifestyle-cities-report-2021](https://www.kaggle.com/datasets/prasertk/healthy-lifestyle-cities-report-2021)
* [https://www.kaggle.com/datasets/tunguz/major-cities-of-the-world](https://www.kaggle.com/datasets/tunguz/major-cities-of-the-world)

The workshop will be recorded and posted with our other workshop recordings on the [R-Ladies St. Louis YouTube](https://www.youtube.com/@rladiesstl7515/videos) page.

title: An basic introduction: Python for R Users
description:
**SPEAKER:**
Wanyin Li is a research student in Psycholinguistics and examines bilingual reading from the University of Birmingham.

**TOPIC OVERVIEW**
I decided to repeat this session at the end of November. Due to the link connection and multiple changes of dates, a few people missed the last session.

R is a language made by and for statisticians, whereas Python is a more general-purpose programming language. Regardless of the background, there will be times when a particular algorithm is implemented in one language and not the other; a feature is better documented.

This workshop helps you cross the R-Python language barrier. Learn about some of the most important data types and data structures in Python and how they compare to the ones in R. We will also cover control flow statements (if-else) for loops and shows you how to write your own functions in Python!

It will be friendly for Python beginners, but we expect you to have some background knowledge of R.

**PREPARATION AND RESOURCES**
There is nothing to prepare in advance.

**ONLINE PROGRAMME**
The event will be held online and will start at 15:00 on 30/11/2023 (GMT).
Please join us via this Zoom link:
[https://bham-ac-uk.zoom.us/j/81953714649?pwd=QzNpaXlMcFpmODFpclFrNFgxdTJQdz09](https://bham-ac-uk.zoom.us/j/81953714649?pwd=QzNpaXlMcFpmODFpclFrNFgxdTJQdz09)

**CODE OF CONDUCT**
Please take a minute to read our code of conduct - we are committed to creating a safe and harassment-free environment:
https://github.com/rladies/.github/blob/master/CODE_OF_CONDUCT.md

R-Ladies events are always free and open to anyone who wants to participate as long as they follow the code of conduct. We are pro-actively inclusive of queer, trans, and all minority identities, with additional sensitivity to intersectional identities. Our priority is to provide a safe community space for anyone identifying as a minority gender who is interested in and/or working with R.

title: Introduction to base R and ggplot2
description:
***\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*IMPORTANT\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\****

**DATE:** TUESDAY, NOVEMBER 28th

**LOCATION:** ROOM 189, 550 RUE SHERBROOKE OUEST (H3A 1B9), IN DOWNTOWN MONTRÉAL

***\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*IMPORTANT\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\****

## Details

Join us on **TUESDAY November 28th** for our final meetup of the year!

**Agenda:**

* Settling in and and request for talks
* Speaker
* Networking / practical coding time

**Topic:**
This month we'll be discussing commonly used base R commands and an introduction to the R package ggplot2 for visualizing data.

This session, we will work through exercises related to:

* essential commands in base R (getting started, handling data, etc.)
* visualizing data in graphs using ggplot2
* exploring resources outside of Rstudio/Posit to support your R learning

While this is a more introductory R meetup, all levels are welcome!
Experienced R-users are encouraged to help new R users!

**What to bring:**
Bring your laptop if you'd like to follow along with the examples. We recommend installing the latest version of [R](https://www.r-project.org/) and [RStudio](https://posit.co/products/open-source/rstudio/) before you arrive.

**Other:**
R-Ladies events always have been and will always be free to attend! RSVPs are not required, but do help us plan space requirements. Please note have changed our location to McGill campus at 550 rue Sherbrooke for this meetup, with space generously provided by the Quantitative Life Sciences department. See above for location details.

**Questions?**

title: R-Ladies Baltimore -- Holiday graphics and animations in R (in person!)
description:
R-Ladies Baltimore (Bmore) is the local Baltimore chapter for the larger R-Ladies global organization (rladies.org) to promote gender diversity in the R community.

The next event is Tuesday November 28, 2023 from 3:30-5pm at FastForward (1812 Ashland Ave #110, Baltimore, MD 21205) at Johns Hopkins Technology Ventures (JHTV). Coffee, tea and cookies will be provided. This meeting will include a group coding event. Pre-pandemic times, we made holiday cards at our November meetup. It was so much fun, that we are going to do it again!

WHO is this for?

* If you are new to R & are short-budgeted on time!
* If you want to learn more about creating data visualizations in R, but don't know how!
* If you want to learn about how to make your own holiday designs in R!

*If any/all of this is true, this event might be for you!*

4:40–5:00 Networking -- Any more Qs?

*To benefit most from this workshop*

2. pre-installed R & RStudio (https://www.rstudio.com/products/rstudio/)
3. tidyverse package (https://www.tidyverse.org/)

Having trouble with installation & set-up? Ping us on Slack!

LOCATION:

Enter the 1812 Ashland building and go straight ahead in lobby past the security desk & elevators to Suite #110/JHTV entrance.

FAQ:

Q: Are men welcome to attend R-Ladies events?

A: Yes, men are welcome at this event. Our mission is to encourage more women and non-binary individuals to get involved in the R community. However, we ask that cis men come as guests of women and gender minorities to our events to promote a sense support and encouragement for women and gender minorities in the R community. Please help us by spreading the word about R-Ladies. Please also take the time to review our code of conduct before you attend: https://github.com/rladies/starter-kit/wiki/Code-of-Conduct

The term "women and gender minorities" is inclusive of individuals who identify as female (trans and cis), trans-men, non-binary, genderqueer, genderfluid, agender, intersex, and all minority genders).

title: Natural language processing in R
description:
In our next meetup we will discuss NLP in R. We will hear two presentations:

* **Florian Schwendinger**: Introduction to NLP in R
* **Camilla Damian**: Detecting Gender Bias in Children's Books

First Flo will give a general overview of the topic and show relevant R packages (see also the CRAN Task View on the topic: https://cran.r-project.org/web/views/NaturalLanguageProcessing.html).

This will be followed by an application described by Camilla:
*Gender stereotypes form early in the child’s development and are carried over throughout adolescence into adulthood, leaving long-lasting effects which may impact activity and career choices, as well as academic performance. Books, in particular, can have considerable influence, as their characters serve to shape role models of femininity and masculinity for young children. Thus, gender under- and misrepresentation in children’s textual literature can contribute to the internalization and reinforcement of stereotypes. To address this issue, we aim to identify and measure relevant dimensions of gender bias in children’s books with the aid of both qualitative and quantitative techniques: systematic literature review across disciplines on the one hand and natural language processing (NLP) methods on the other. By exploiting such an integrated research framework, we believe that it is possible to automate the detection of potentially biased text while enhancing the interpretability and transparency of the results.*

description:

https://github.com/RLadiesCuerna/meetup_noviembre_2023

Para aprovechar al máximo este Meetup, se recomienda tener conocimientos básicos de R y RStudio.

Tener instalado R >= 4.0
Tener instalado RStudio >= 1.4

ggplot2, gplots, vegan, reshape2, circlize, ComplexHeatmap, Heatplus
Pueden instalarlas con las siguientes instrucciones:

CRAN
install.packages("ggplot2")

Bioconductor
BiocManager::install("ComplexHeatmap")
BiocManager::install("Heatplus")

title: {glitter} un Package R pour Explorer et Collecter des Données du Web Sémantique
description:
R-Ladies Paris vous donne rendez-vous le mardi 14 novembre à partir de 12H30 en direct sur ZOOM. Notre invité Lise Vaudor va nous présenter le package {glitter} (https://lvaudor.github.io/glitter/).

**Description de l’intervention :**

Pour collecter des données à partir de sources web sémantiques telles que Wikidata, dbpedia, HAL, et d'autres, il est en principe nécessaire de formuler des requêtes en SPARQL. Ce langage de requête est au web sémantique ce que SQL est aux bases de données relationnelles, mais il reste encore largement méconnu des utilisateur·rice·s de données. Au contraire, R est largement utilisé en tant que langage de programmation généraliste offrant une grande polyvalence pour gérer divers aspects de la chaîne de traitement des données.

Le package glitter permet de :

\- générer des requêtes SPARQL en s'appuyant sur des commandes R inspirées du tidyverse\,
\- les envoyer aux points d'accès de leur choix\,
\- recueillir les données sous forme tabulaire\.
Ces étapes sont ainsi intégrées de manière transparente dans l'environnement R, permettant aux utilisateur·rice·s d'effectuer des analyses avancées et de valoriser les données de manière reproductible.

Lors de cette présentation, nous explorerons les principales fonctionnalités du package glitter en utilisant des exemples concrets.

Veuillez vous inscrire à notre événement à travers ce lien 🔗 [https://us06web.zoom.us/meeting/register/tZcpdemuqjwiH9MexfvujTnzwZXNuPw5hdlF](https://us06web.zoom.us/meeting/register/tZcpdemuqjwiH9MexfvujTnzwZXNuPw5hdlF)

Nous sommes impatients de vous voir nombreux à cet événement ! 👥

title: (Even More) Exciting Data Visualizations with ggplot2 Extensions
description:
This time we will have **[Cédric Scherer](https://www.cedricscherer.com/)**, who is a **data visualization designer**, **consultant**, and **instructor** helping clients and workshop participants to create engaging and effective graphics.

Creating effective and easily accessible data visualizations of high quality in an efficient and preferably reproducible way is an essential skill for everyone working in a data-related field.

Luckily, by leveraging the functionality of ggplot2, R users can create highly customized graphics without the need for post-processing — and a huge number of extension packages allows you to create even more exciting visualizations and improve your workflow.

In this session, **Cédric** will give an overview of the world of **ggplot2** extension packages and illustrate their usage and benefits by walking you through some examples of more advanced data visualizations and less common chart types.

**Introduction to Observable Plot**

Discover the power of interactive data visualization with Observable Plot, an easy-to-use JavaScript library. This is a beginner friendly talk, no coding required. Together we’ll explore how you can build your own visualizations directly in Observable notebooks and how you can integrate your visuals in Quarto. Data enthusiasts of all levels welcome!

**Presented by Tanya Shapiro**

title: R-Ladies Ottawa Online Lightning Talks
description:
**R-Ladies Ottawa Online Lightning Talks**

Join R-Ladies Ottawa for an online presentation!

**What is it?**

Join R-Ladies Ottawa for three virtual presentations from our committee!

**1: Recreating a visualization with ggplot2**
*Alex McSween & Deirdre Hennessey, Statistics Canada*

In 2021 Canada became the first country to provide census data on transgender and non-binary people. In this presentation we’ll use openly available data, R, and Python to create visualizations inspired by those released [by Statistics Canada](https://www150.statcan.gc.ca/n1/daily-quotidien/220427/dq220427b-eng.htm).

**2: How to use ChatGPT + GitHub Copilot to help you learn R**
*Melissa Van Bussel, Statistics Canada*

Generative AI is a great tool for learning R, whether you’re a beginner to the language or already an R expert. In this demo, we’ll see how ChatGPT and GitHub Copilot can be used directly from within RStudio to help make writing R code a lot easier.

**3: What an early 2000s reality show taught me about file management**
*Reiko Okamoto, Bruyere Hospital*

Ideas from home organization shows are surprisingly applicable to file management. Using a room divider to establish dedicated zones for different activities in a studio apartment is analogous to creating self-contained projects in RStudio. Likewise, swapping mismatched hangers with matching ones to tidy a closet resembles the adoption of a file naming convention to make a directory easier to navigate.

In this talk, I will share good practices in file management through the lens of home organization. We all know that clutter, whether it is in our physical space or on our machine, destroys our ability to focus. These practices will help R users of all levels create a serene, relaxing environment where they feel inspired to work with data.

**When and Where?**

The event will take place online at 12pm EST on November 9th. Please register in our meetup to receive the zoom link.

**Need more info?**

For any questions or comments please reach out to [ottawa@rladies.org.](mailto:ottawa@rladies.org)

**Discussions éclair en ligne de R-Ladies Ottawa**

Rejoignez R-Ladies Ottawa pour une présentation en ligne !

**Qu'est-ce que c'est?**

Rejoignez R-Ladies Ottawa pour trois présentations virtuelles de notre comité !

**1: Recréer une visualisation avec ggplot2**
*Alex McSween et Deirdre Hennessey, Statistique Canada*

En 2021, le Canada est devenu le premier pays à fournir des données de recensement sur les personnes transgenres et non binaires. Dans cette présentation, nous utiliserons des données librement disponibles, R et Python pour créer des visualisations inspirées de celles publiées par Statistique Canada.

**2: Comment utiliser ChatGPT + GitHub Copilot pour vous aider à apprendre R**
*Melissa Van Bussel, Statistique Canada*

L'IA générative est un excellent outil pour apprendre R, que vous soyez débutant dans le langage ou déjà expert en R. Dans cette démo, nous verrons comment ChatGPT et GitHub Copilot peuvent être utilisés directement depuis RStudio pour faciliter grandement l'écriture de code R.

**3: Ce qu'une émission de télé-réalité du début des années 2000 m’a appris sur la gestion des fichiers**
*Reiko Okamoto, Hôpital Bruyère*

Idées des émissions de l’organisation de la maison sont étonnamment applicable à la gestion des fichiers. L'utilisation d'une cloison pour établir des zones dédiées à différentes activités dans un appartement studio est analogue à créer des projets autonomes dans RStudio. De même, remplacer de cintres dépareillés avec lesquels qui correspondent pour ordonner un placard ressemble à l’adoption d’une convention d'appellation des fichiers pour faciliter la navigation dans un répertoire.

Dans cette discussion, je partagerai les bonnes pratiques en matière de gestion des fichiers par l'angle de l’organisation de la maison. Nous savons tous que le désordre, si c’est dans notre espace physique ou sur notre machine, détruit notre capacité à nous concentrer. Ces pratiques aideront les utilisateurs de R de tous niveaux à créer un environnement serein et relaxant où ils se sentent inspirés pour travailler avec des données.

**Quand et où?**

L'événement aura lieu en ligne à 12 h HNE le 9 novembre. Veuillez vous inscrire à notre rencontre pour recevoir le lien zoom.

**Besoin de plus d'informations ?**

Pour toute question ou commentaire, veuillez contacter [ottawa@rladies.org.](mailto:ottawa@rladies.org)

title: Introduction to Network Analysis in R
description:
Please join us for an introduction to social network analysis in R. For this workshop we welcome [Dr. Leslie Echols](https://psychology.missouristate.edu/profile-display.aspx?p=LeslieEchols) from Missouri State University. Leslie has [expertise](https://scholar.google.com/citations?hl=en&user=YuFLyNkAAAAJ&view_op=list_works&sortby=pubdate) in social network analysis and its applications to peer relationships, especially relationships among middle-school age children.

Install the **statnet** package ahead of time to follow along.

title: Teaching Computers to See Scatterplots with Scagnostics
description:
Hello friends! It has been a while since our last event and we are excited to announce our next R-Ladies event! Come and join us to learn about how computers can see scatterplots! This will be a hybid event and zoomlink will be posted prior to the event.

As the number of dimensions in a data set increases, visualising its structure and variable dependencies becomes more tedious. Scagnostics (scatterplot diagnostics) are numerical measures that describe the visual features of scatter plots. These features can be used to identify interesting and abnormal scatterplots within high-dimensional datasets and thus give a sense of priority to the variables we choose to visualise. A set of scagnostics is implemented in the `cassowaryr` R package, which provides a user-friendly method to apply these diagnostics to data. The scagnostics have been effective tools in both high-dimensional data visualisation and projection pursuit, which Harriet will illustrate with examples from sports and astrophysics.

title: Palestra: Programa de Mentoria da R-Ladies Goiânia na UFG
description:
A R-Ladies Goiânia não para!!! É com grande satisfação que anuanciamos nossa parceria com a Universidade Federal de Goiás (UFG) para oferecer uma valiosa mentoria online aos alunos do curso de graduação em Estatística.

Esta parceria reflete nosso compromisso com o desenvolvimento educacional e profissional dos gêneros subrepresentados, na comunidade R. Sendo esta uma ferramenta poderosa para análise estatística e visualização de dados.

Estamos entusiasmados em fornecer orientação e suporte práticos aos alunos da UFG, compartilhando nosso conhecimento e experiência em estatística com o R. Acreditamos que esta colaboração contribuirá para o aprimoramento das habilidades dos estudantes, preparando-os para um futuro de sucesso na área de estatística e análise de dados.

Venha assistir a nossa palestra na UFG.
Espero vocês!

title: Preparando tarjetas y curriculums
description: Juntas vamos a explorar opciones de tarjetas de Navidad y currículums, que expondremos en nuestra siguiente reunión.

title: 4 days workshop
description:
We are holding a four day workshop.We will learn about the R language, its significance, and some of its key packages in these workshops. We will also gain knowledge of data impotation and basic mathematical computation. At last, we'll make a tale from it using various visualization tools.
Participants must bring their laptop, chargers,pens and notebooks.

title: How ChatGPT may affect the future of Data Science
description: In collaboration with R-Ladies Accra and Botswana R User Group, we will look at how the field of Artificial Intelligence (AI) is rapidly evolving, captivating and challenging us with the potential for machines to learn and create. One intriguing advancement in this domain is Generative AI, which has the power to significantly impact programming and data science. In this talk, Ardalan will share what he has learnt from creating DataMotto, a proactive AI for data analysis, and delve into the world of Large Language models, the engine behind tools like ChatGPT. We will explore what Large Language models are, how they are influencing data science, the challenges they present, and the lessons learned from developing a tool that utilizes this innovative technology. Whether you are a data enthusiast or simply curious about AI, join us in this discussion as we delve into the exciting intersection of Generative AI and data science.

title: How ChatGPT may affect the future of Data Science
description:
This is a collaboration between R-Ladies Gaborone and R-Ladies Accra. The speaker,Dr Ardalan Mirshani will discuss how the rapidly evolving field of Artificial Intelligence (AI) intersect with Data Science.
Join us on November 4, 2023 for an interesting and informative discussion.

title: Let's go to the Zurich R User Group event together!

title: 旅遊服務銜接 AIGC 的各種坑
description:
**[Agenda]**

• 19:30 \~ 19:40 (10 mins) 開場

• 19:40 \~ 20:40 (60 mins) 主議程 : 旅遊服務銜接 AIGC 的各種坑

• 20:40 \~ 自由交流時間

**[Talk Information]**

聊聊真程旅行社嘗試將旅遊服務和AIGC- AI Generated Content（也就是那個超級酷炫的人工智慧）結合時，會遇到的一些...(嗯 其實很多)，讓人啼笑皆非的坑。

超級依賴症：當我們完全依賴AI建議行程，結果它帶你去了一家已經關門大吉的餐廳。旅行社可以跟客人一起嘆氣說：“哎，我知道我不該那麼懶...”嗎?

路線的驚喜：AI可能會安排：“我們要太魯閣玩完去住逢甲夜市的飯店！”，這可以排嗎?

無字天書猜猜看：有資料才能計算，資料的清理永遠是第一個大魔王。客人輸入的文字SML，SL market是甚麼?

title: RLadies October Meetup - "R-omics": Using R for bioinformatics
description:
Join us on October 30th for our second meetup of the 2023-24 season!

**Agenda:**

* Settling in and and request for talks
* Speaker
* Networking / practical coding time

**Topic:**
This month we'll be discussing how to use R for bioinformatics. Bioinformatics is a rapidly evolving field that combines aspects of data science, biostatistics, and natural sciences in order to analyze biological datasets. Many research areas can fall under the umbrella of bioinformatics, from modelling the 3D structure of proteins to identifying gene mutations associated with disease.

This session, we will work through exercises related to:

* essential resources for working with genomic data, like Bioconductor packages
* visualizing high-dimensional data such as spatial transcriptomics
* calling command-line tools from R

While some biology background will be helpful for this session, everyone is welcome.

**What to bring:**
Bring your laptop if you'd like to follow along with the examples. We recommend installing the latest version of [R](https://www.r-project.org/) and [RStudio](https://posit.co/products/open-source/rstudio/) before you arrive.

**Other:**
R-Ladies events always have been and will always be free to attend! RSVPs are not required, but do help us plan space requirements.

**Questions?**
Please comment or message the organizers with any questions.

title: R-Ladies Abuja and Abuja R user Group Meet and Greet
description:
Calling all R enthusiasts based in Abuja--let's chat! We are hosting a low-key event to get to know ourselves. During our Meet and greet, we'll:

* **introduce ourselves,**
* **have lunch,**
* **have some R fun, and**
* **discuss things like how we can support ourselves - career, skills development, book clubs, how should meet, and where, and much more.**

**What to Expect:**
🌟 *Storytelling Circle:* In this safe and welcoming space, we encourage you to share your unique R and professional story, challenges, and triumphs. Let's uplift one another by listening and learning from the diverse experiences.
🌟 *Networking Opportunities:* Connect with like-minds who are making waves. Expand your circle of support, collaborate on new projects, and find potential mentors or mentees.
🌟 *Empowering Activities:* Engage in fun and empowering activities that encourage personal growth and self-discovery. Together, we'll explore ways to harness our strengths and make a positive impact.
**Who Should Attend:**
This event is open to all students and professionals who are passionate about R programming and technology in general. Whether you're just starting your journey or are already a trailblazer, we welcome you with open arms. This inclusive event welcomes professionals of all backgrounds, ages, and experiences. We are committed to creating a safe and respectful environment for all participants.
**Why Attend:**
🌻 *Form Genuine Connections:* Build authentic relationships with students and professionals who share your commitment to growth.
🌻 *Supportive Community:* Become part of a nurturing community that celebrates your victories and supports you through professional and academic challenges.
🌻 *Unleash Your Potential:* Discover new career possibilities, find your voice, and unlock your full potential in a supportive environment.
Let's kickstart a wave of exciting gatherings together – come on over, grab a drink, and let's connect! 🍻🍷✨ We can't wait to meet you.

title: Meetup R-Ladies São Paulo - Introdução ao R
description:
Trata-se de de uma oficina de um dia inteiro, gratuita e que não exige conhecimento prévio de R ou outra linguagem de programação.

**Público-alvo: pessoas que se identificam como minoria de gênero (mulher trans ou cis, homem trans, pessoa não-binária, queer).**

Nesta edição contaremos com o espaço **GuGuDaDados,** para incentivar a presença de mães e cuidadores em nossos eventos\*\*!\*\* É um espaço colaborativo e experimental que as RLadies propõem com o objetivo de deixar as crianças entretidas e cuidadas enquanto as pessoas responsáveis podem se dedicar com tranquilidade à sua formação.

Também teremos um lanche colaborativo!

Por favor leia todas as informações abaixo!

• **O QUE PRECISO FAZER PARA PARTICIPAR?**

* Passo 1: faça aqui sua pré inscrição no meetup - você ficará na lista de espera e tudo bem, é isso mesmo!
* Passo 2: você deverá preencher [este fomulário](https://docs.google.com/forms/d/e/1FAIpQLSdarGAYPo_2Tc83qZqUytg2gccpAfsJ7oNzVqmg1pnMYZ9HPQ/viewform): (que também enviaremos por email, então, nos dê seu email ;-)
Link: https://shorturl.at/wB249
* Passo 3: esperar! Quando sua inscrição for realizada, você entrará na lista de presença do meetup.
* Passo 4: Você receberá um email com o status da sua inscrição - cheque seu email com frequência!

**Por que estamos fazendo isso?**
*Temos vagas reservadas para pessoas negras e indígenas, vagas reservadas para mães e vagas prioritárias para minorias de gênero.*
*E como nesta plataforma (MeetUp) não conseguimos captar essas informações, estamos experimentando este processo para garantir maior diversidade do nosso público alvo.*

• **O QUE PRECISO FAZER PARA PARTICIPAR?**

* Seguir o passo-a-passo acima
* Ler o código de conduta (mais detalhes abaixo)
* Caso seja possível, levar seu próprio notebook (e, se der, sua própria caneca!)
* **Instalar em seu notebook os programas R e RStudio ANTES do curso**: [https://www.curso-r.com/material/instalacao/](https://www.curso-r.com/material/instalacao/)
(Se não conseguir, não se preocupe que te ajudamos no dia!)
* Vontade de aprender R em um ambiente inclusivo, amistoso e seguro :)

• CÓDIGO DE CONDUTA (disponível aqui: [https://guide.rladies.org/about/coc/#portuguese](https://guide.rladies.org/about/coc/#portuguese))
Qualquer pessoa que violar ou houver previamente violado o código de conduta desta comunidade terá sua entrada proibida, será expulsa/o e/ou estará sujeita/o à ação que a pessoa organizadora achar mais apropriada, independente de inscrição no evento, ser membro do MeetUp ou qualquer outra condição.

***

• CRONOGRAMA DO CURSO:
https://r-ladies-sao-paulo.github.io/2023-06-oficina-introducao-R/

• PALESTRANTES
\- Bianca Muniz
\- Geovana Lopes
\- Haydee Svab
\- Renata Hirota

***

• APOIO:

* Insper (cessão do espaço)
* R Consortium (apoio financeiro)
* Curso-R (bolsas em cursos para sorteio)

***

• SOBRE AS R-LADIES
R-Ladies é uma organização que promove a diversidade de gênero na comunidade da linguagem R. Nosso principal objetivo é promover a linguagem computacional estatística R compartilhando conhecimento, assim, quem tiver interesse na linguagem será bem-vinda, independente do nível de conhecimento. Nosso principal público-alvo são as pessoas que se identificam com o gênero feminino, portanto, mulheres cis, mulheres trans, bem como pessoas não-binárias e queer.

***

O curso é gratuito, mas as vagas são limitadas. Seja consciente e só se inscreva se puder comparecer ao evento.

title: Career Development Talk
description:
At our upcoming event, we're putting the spotlight on **careers in data science and statistics**. We want you to be a part of this enriching conversation. If you attend, you'll not only have the **opportunity** to absorb valuable insights but also to actively **engage**. We encourage you to ask questions, **seek advice**, and share your own **career journey**.

Your unique story and questions are invaluable contributions to the discussion, making this event a dynamic exchange of ideas and experiences. We look forward to hearing your voice and helping you navigate the exciting world of data science and statistics careers.

title: Introducción a R
description:
Este taller está diseñado para principiantes en el lenguaje de programación estadística por computadora R. Aprenderemos R desde cero. Sin embargo puedes intentar descargar e instalar el software en tu computadora personal, te dejo aquí el link:
https://rstudio.com/products/rstudio/download/

El programa está basado en el libro:
"R para principiantes" de Emmanuel Paradis, este libro enfoca sus conocimientos y comandos en R base. Los temas que se tocarán son:

Rbase y RStudio
Instalación y paqueterías
Objetos en R
Importar tus datos a R
Manipulación de objetos
Operadores
Funciones aritméticas
Ejemplo de análisis estadístico

En cuanto al material que necesitaremos para desarrollar la clase, lo liberaremos 24 horas antes de nuestro evento.
Muchas gracias a todos por participar!
Disfruten

title: R-Ladies Baltimore -- Visualization and analysis of spatial data (Virtual)
description:
R-Ladies Baltimore (Bmore) is excited to host a meetup on Friday October 27, 2023 from 4-5:30pm (entirely virtual meetup on Zoom). We will have a wonderful presentation from Yi Wang, a fifth-year PhD student in the Johns Hopkins Department of Biostatistics. She will be giving a talk on "Visualization and analysis of spatial data"!

WHO is this for?

* If you are new to R and want to learn about packages to visualize spatial data?
* If you know R, but want to expand your knowledge spatial packages?

*If any/all of this is true, this event might be for you!*

AGENDA

* 4:00–4:15pm Who we are \| Announcements \| What's next
* 4:15–5 pm Presentation: Visualization and analysis of spatial data
* 5–5:30pm Networking -- Any more Qs?

Details on the location:

* The Zoom information will be sent to those who RSVP the week before the event.

FAQ:

Q: Are men welcome to attend R-Ladies events?

A: Yes, men are welcome at this event. Our mission is to encourage more women and non-binary individuals to get involved in the R community. However, we ask that cis men come as guests of women and gender minorities to our events to promote a sense support and encouragement for women and gender minorities in the R community. Please help us by spreading the word about R-Ladies. Please also take the time to review our code of conduct before you attend: https://github.com/rladies/starter-kit/wiki/Code-of-Conduct

The term "women and gender minorities" is inclusive of individuals who identify as female (trans and cis), trans-men, non-binary, genderqueer, genderfluid, agender, intersex, and all minority genders).

Important to know:

* If this is your first R-Ladies event, please take a moment to review our R-Ladies Global code of conduct: https://github.com/rladies/starter-kit/wiki/Code-of-Conduct

Finally, for more information:

title: 5º Meetup Virtual R_Ladies Salvador
description:
Olá, pessoal! 🌐💜

📣 Participe do Minicurso de Introdução à Linguagem R! 📊

Você está pronto para dar os primeiros passos no emocionante mundo da análise de dados? O Minicurso de Introdução à Linguagem R é a sua oportunidade perfeita para começar 💜

🌐 Introdução ao R: Descubra por que o R é uma das linguagens mais poderosas para análise de dados. Aprenda a instalar, configurar e começar a usar o R para resolver problemas reais.

📈 Manipulação de Dados: Dominar a manipulação de dados é fundamental para qualquer analista de dados. Neste minicurso, você aprenderá técnicas essenciais para importar, limpar e transformar dados de forma eficaz utilizando o #dplyr.

🗓️ Quando: 25/10/2023 das 18h30 às 21h30

📍 Faça sua inscrição no link a seguir! 😉

**[Link inscrição](https://forms.gle/Tcbeb4P57MiX1EEn9)**

Projeto de Extensão: Diversidade de gênero no aprendizado do R

🎓 Camila é formada em Estatística pela UFBA, mestranda em Matemática com área de concentração em Estatística na UFBA e é analista de dados da SEI Bahia - Superintendência de Estudos Econômicos e Sociais da Bahia.

💡 Não é necessário experiência anterior em programação ou análise de dados. Este minicurso é perfeito para iniciantes!

🚀 Prepare-se para decolar em sua jornada de análise de dados com o R!

📝 Inscrições abertas agora. Vagas limitadas! Garanta o seu lugar. Acesse o [link de inscrição](https://forms.gle/Tcbeb4P57MiX1EEn9).

Não perca esta oportunidade de adquirir habilidades valiosas em análise de dados com R. Junte-se a nós e comece a transformar dados em insights!

title: RLadies - PyLadies Paris Afterwork Meetup
description:
**🇺🇸** \-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\- Read in English below \-\-\-\-\-\-\-\- **🇺🇸**
(This event will be conducted in both French and English.)

**🇫🇷 --------- version en français --------- 🇫🇷**

👋 Bonjour à toutes et à tous !

On apprécie toujours les rencontres ensemble, mais c’est encore mieux de voir nos ami(e)s de la communauté PyLadies!

Venez discuter de R, de Python et d'autres sujets passionnants, et rencontrer des personnes partageant votre passion pour la technologie en région parisienne !

Pour ce meetup, nous vous donnons rendez-vous dans un bar près d'Opéra à Paris. Pour connaître le nom et l'adresse du bar, nous vous invitons à vous inscrire à l'événement.

Nous ne pourrons pas offrir l'apéritif, cependant, nous vous invitons à acheter au moins une boisson de votre choix au bar qui nous accueille.

Veuillez noter que cet événement est organisé par PyLadies et RLadies. Bien que tout le monde soit le bienvenu, si vous ne vous identifiez pas en tant que femme, nous vous encourageons à inviter une femme à vous accompagner.

**Nous avons réservé un espace dans le bar. Nous vous prions donc de mettre à jour votre RSVP pour confirmer ou annuler votre participation.**

Toutes les personnes participant à cet événement sont tenues de respecter le code de conduite du groupe : [https://github.com/R-Ladies-Paris/Code-of-Conduct](https://github.com/R-Ladies-Paris/Code-of-Conduct)

Nous avons hâte de vous voir,
Chaïma, Chiara, Maria, Mojdeh, Mouna

**🇺🇸 --------- description in English --------- 🇺🇸**

👋 Hello everyone,

It is great meeting each other, but even better together with our friends from the PyLadies community.

Come, chat about anything R or Python related or just meet other Tech lovers in the Paris area!

The meetup will be at a bar in central Paris, near Opera. You can view the name and address once you have registered for the event.

We will not be providing food or drinks. It would be appreciated if everyone could buy at least one drink for themselves.

Note that this is a PyLadies and RLadies event. Everyone is welcome, however, if you don't identify as a woman, we encourage you to invite a woman to join you as a +1.

**Please make sure to update your registration status if you decide to join or cancel your participation, as we have booked a space for us in the bar.**

All the participants are asked to adhere to our code of conduct: [https://github.com/R-Ladies-Paris/Code-of-Conduct](https://github.com/R-Ladies-Paris/Code-of-Conduct)

Can't wait to see you there,
Yours,
Chaïma, Chiara, Maria, Mojdeh, Mouna

title: Reproducible pipelines book club #1
description:
Welcome to the first meeting of our book club! We will be going through the chapters of [“Building reproducible analytical pipelines with R”](https://raps-with-r.dev/) by Bruno Rodrigues.

In this meeting, Julia Romanowska (R-Ladies Bergen) will go through the first three chapters, which are a nice introduction to the topic, and discuss why should we care about reproducibility in the first place.

This will be an online event, and you are welcome to code along and discuss any questions that might arise. We are all learning together!

The meeting will not be recorded.

title: Reproducible pipelines book club #1
description:
First meeting in our book club! We will be going through the chapters of [“Building reproducible analytical pipelines with R”](https://raps-with-r.dev/) by Bruno Rodrigues.

During this first meeting, Julia Romanowska from R-Ladies Bergen will go through chapters 1-3, which make a nice introduction to why do we need to care about reproducibility at all. This will be an online event where you are welcome to code along and discuss any questions or problems that might arise. We are all learning this together!

The meeting will not be recorded.

title: Zero to Quarto: Build your first Quarto website
description:
**Zero to Quarto: build your first Quarto website!**

**Quarto** is one of the most discussed topics in the R community for the past year. In the posit conf 2023 that just happened a few weeks ago, there were 23 (!) talks on this **open-source publishing system**. You can build a personal or organisation website; a course or workshop website; a blog; a scientific report; a presentation; all with the possibility to display and execute code (R, Python, Julia), and for free.

If you:

* don't know what quarto is yet, and want to find out more
* heard of quarto somewhere, but don't know where to start
* are an expert in quarto and want to share your experience

then this workshop is for you!

Join us and our speaker **Chi Zhang** on **Monday, October 23rd** for this exciting event.

**No previous experience with Quarto is required**, just bring your laptop and charger and join us for this creative programming experience ;-)

Wi-Fi will be provided at the event location.

Materials for the workshop can be downloaded or forked from
[https://andreaczhang.github.io/workshop-02quarto/](https://andreaczhang.github.io/workshop-02quarto/)

Program:
18:00 - 18:30 possibility to get support for installing the packages and getting the material necessary for the workshop. If you are an R-beginner and need any type of tech support, don't hesitate to ask us!
18:30 - 18:40 welcome and some ice-breaker questions
18:45 - 19:30 workshop
19:30 onwards: socializing with some drinks at TBD

title: [ONLINE] Debugging in R with Shannon Pileggi
description:
Please join us for our next meetup with Shannon Pileggi member of R-Ladies Philly on **Debugging in R**!

**Topic:** In this workshop, we will review code troubleshooting tips, discuss debugging functions (*traceback()*, *browser()*, *debug()*, *trace()*, and *recover()*), and distinguish between strategies for debugging your own code versus someone else’s code.

**Format:** This will be a hands-on workshop in Posit (RStudio) Cloud. This is a special instance of RStudio that you access through your browser. We will share details on how to log in to Posit Cloud to registered participants closer to the event.

**Agenda:** We will open the Zoom room to mingle and make sure everyone has access to Posit Cloud. The workshop will start at 15 minutes past the advertised start time. We expect this workshop to end at 8pm ET.

**Pre-requisites:** Knowledge of how to write a function in R.

**Learning objectives:**

* Review code troubleshooting tips.
* Discuss debugging functions (traceback(), browser(), debug(), trace(), and recover()) and the additional benefits of employing some of these strategies within RStudio.
* Distinguish between strategies for debugging your own code versus someone else’s code.

**Slides:** [https://shannonpileggi.github.io/debugging-nhsr/#/title-slide](https://shannonpileggi.github.io/debugging-nhsr/#/title-slide)

**Code of Conduct:** All events are intended for women as leaders, mentors, members, and attendees. We are emphatically queer and trans friendly. Men are welcomed to attend *as guests* and must be accompanied by the R-Lady who invited them. This group was created so those who identify as women have a comfortable place to learn. If you would like to attend and feel excluded, please introduce yourself to an organizer.

**Contact:** If you have any questions or concerns, please reach out to organizer, Sheila Saia, on meetup.

title: R-Ladies Coffe and Code Hangout
description:
Come join R-Ladies NY for an afternoon of coding and coffee. This will be an informal, hands-on working session where we'll work together on our own projects or tutorials.

Come in curious about a subject or looking to make progress on a project you're working on!

title: Trabalhando com R no Jornalismo de Dados
description:
**Descrição:** No workshop "Jornalismo de Dados com R" vamos explorar o uso do software R no jornalismo, acompanhando a produção de um relatório com Quarto que destrincha o fluxo de trabalho no jornalismo de dados. Serão mostradas referências, técnicas e pacotes que mais ajudam a analisar e visualizar informações durante uma apuração jornalística.

**Link do evento:** O link do evento será disponibilizado após a inscrição. Atenção, próximo da data do evento, você receberá uma senha de acesso pelo email. Verifique a sua caixa de Spam. Você também pode nos contactar pela seção "Comentários" aqui na página do evento ou escrever para ribeirao-preto@rladies.org.

**Sobre as RLadies:** Somos uma organização sem fins lucrativos que atua em diversos países. A nossa missão é promover o uso da linguagem R entre grupos minorizados incluindo mulheres cis, mulheres trans, pessoas não-binárias, entre outros grupos subrepresentados para uma comunidade mais diversa e inclusiva.

[http://www.rladies.org/](http://www.rladies.org/)

**Código de Conduta:** Nós das RLadies, oferecemos treinamento e mentoria em um ambiente amigável, seguro e inclusivo. O nosso código de conduta aplica-se a todos os espaços RLadies, tanto no virtual como no presencial. Para mais informações, leia o nosso código de conduta.

[https://guide.rladies.org/about/coc/](https://guide.rladies.org/about/coc/)

title: Taller de Estadística Descriptiva en R
description:
**¡Únete a nosotros, R-Ladies Milagro (ECU)**, en la celebración del Día de la Estadística en nuestro emocionante taller **"Taller de Estadística Descriptiva en R"**. Prepárate para sumergirte en un mundo fascinante y descubre la belleza que se oculta en los números y la verdad que se esconde en los datos.

La estadística descriptiva es una herramienta poderosa que nos permite comprender, resumir y visualizar datos de manera efectiva. En este taller, aprenderás a analizar datos de manera descriptiva, explorando conceptos como medidas de tendencia central, dispersión, visualización de datos y más. No importa si eres un principiante en estadística o un experto, este taller te ayudará a desarrollar habilidades valiosas para tomar decisiones informadas en tu vida personal y profesional.

No te pierdas esta oportunidad de descubrir el mundo de la estadística y desbloquear el potencial oculto de los datos. ¡Inscríbete ahora y únete a nosotros en esta emocionante aventura estadística! ¡**Te esperamos con entusiasmo!**

**• Requisitos:**
Asegúrate de tener instalado R y RStudio (Posit)

***NOTA:*** Para participar, debes darle click "Asistiré" en este evento.

title: Retour d'Expériences en Science des Données et en Consultation Scientifique
description:
Nous sommes ravis de vous convier à notre prochain événement. Nous aurons le plaisir d’accueillir en personne Kim Antunez et en visioconférence Anna Doizy. Rendez-vous le 19 octobre 2023 à partir de 19h dans les locaux de datacraft ([https://datacraft.paris/](https://datacraft.paris/)) situés au 13 Rue des Arquebusiers, 75003 Paris. Vous pouvez trouver l'adresse sur Google Maps à cette adresse : [https://maps.app.goo.gl/4nmVrr95VGi7eLne9?g_st=ic](https://maps.app.goo.gl/4nmVrr95VGi7eLne9?g_st=ic)

Le programme de l’événement est le suivant :

**19h - 19h05 :** Introduction de la communauté R-Ladies Paris
**19h05 - 19h35 :** « Mon roman d’appRentissage » présenté par Kim Antunez
**19h35 - 20h05 :** « Freelance et R-lady : un duo gagnant pour la démarche expérimentale » présenté par Anna Doizy
**20h05 - 20h10 :** Mot de clôture
**20h10 - 20h30 :** Réception et échange entre la communauté avec distribution de goodies R

Vous trouverez plus de détails sur nos intervenantes et leurs présentations ci-dessous.

**Biographie :**
Kim Antunez ([https://www.linkedin.com/in/kantunez/](https://www.linkedin.com/in/kantunez/)) est statisticienne publique. Investie depuis 8 ans dans le logiciel R et sa communauté, elle s'intéresse en particulier à l'analyse spatiale, à la datavisualisation et plus largement aux méthodes quantitatives appliquées aux sciences sociales. Convaincue par l’open-data, l’open-source, et l'automatisation de processus statistiques utiles à différents types d'acteurs, elle a réalisé des projets en ce sens, tant professionnels que personnels.

**Titre de la première intervention :**
« Mon roman d’appRentissage »

**Description de l’intervention :**
« Le roman d'apprentissage, ou initiatique, est un genre littéraire ayant pour thème le cheminement d'un héros, souvent jeune, qui atteint progressivement l'idéal de l'humain accompli, faisant l'expérience des grands événements de l'existence : l'amour, l'altérité, la frustration, etc.
L’histoire que je vais vous conter est la mienne : les huit années passées à me former progressivement à la science des données : l’amour de R, son altérité = sa communauté, la frustration de ne pas voir tout le monde s’émerveiller face à l’évolution des logiciels statistiques, pourtant si excitante.
Je vous décrirai les déclics issus de mes projets professionnels et personnels, et comment ils ont fait évoluer le regard que j’ai sur mon métier et ses outils :
\- des packages… autour du lissage spatial et de l'évolution de la géographie communale\.
\- des formations…\. à l’analyse spatiale\, à la cartographie\, d’introduction à R\.
\- des applications shiny… de visualisation de données d’enquêtes\, de simulations de parties de mastermind\, d’invitation à un mariage\.
\- et\, avant tout\, des rencontres \! Hâte de faire la vôtre le 19 octobre prochain \!
»

**Biographie :**
Passionnée par la science, la biologie et l’environnement, je me suis prise d’amour en 2017 pour un caillou au milieu de l’Océan Indien, La Réunion.
Depuis, je suis chercheuse, consultante et formatrice en méthodologie expérimentale et analyses de données avec R.
En 2020, j'ai créé mon entreprise ([https://doana-r.com/](https://doana-r.com/)), je suis indépendante et j'adore ça !
Je travaille principalement avec des chercheurs en biologie et agronomie et je commence à ouvrir mes horizons vers d'autres champs de recherche.

**Titre de la deuxième intervention :**
« Freelance et R-lady : un duo gagnant pour la démarche expérimentale »

**Description de l’intervention :**
« Dans cette présentation, je partagerai mes projets scientifiques et mon expérience en tant qu'indépendante depuis 3 ans et demi. Je raconterai comment j'ai changé de posture en passant de "biostatisticienne" à "consultante scientifique".
Je discuterai en particulier des défis que j'ai rencontrés pour structurer mon entreprise et aider les chercheurs à améliorer leur démarche expérimentale. »

Veuillez vous inscrire à notre événement à travers ce lien 🔗 [https://us06web.zoom.us/meeting/register/tZwscuCppjwrHNMCmyEXSxBF6rqDJ2Wn8z_Z](https://us06web.zoom.us/meeting/register/tZwscuCppjwrHNMCmyEXSxBF6rqDJ2Wn8z_Z)

Si vous prévoyez de participer à notre événement en personne, veuillez ignorer les informations relatives au lien Zoom que vous allez recevoir par e-mail après votre inscription.

Nous vous attendons nombreux !

description:
¡Nuevo meetup! y estamos muy emocionadas :D de contar con dos destacad@s profesionales del mundo de datos, Frank Villafuerte y Mariela Sánchez.
Frank Villafuerte, es licenciado en Estadística por la Universidad Nacional Mayor de San Marcos, actualmente se desempeña como Senior Data Scientist en Caja Arequipa, fue ganador del Hacka Entel 2022 - 2do Puesto y fue Top 10 en Data Challenge 2022 del BBVA. title: An Introduction to targets for R
description:
Hi R-Ladies SB!

We are so excited to announce our next workshop: **An Introduction to targets for R**, with Tracey Mangin!

Interested in clean, reproducible workflows in R? Ever get lost in your own workflow? Have you suffered because you changed part of your workflow or one of your inputs but forgot to incorporate it when producing your results somewhere else? Great news, targets can help. In this session we will introduce the R package targets, which is a pipeline tool that coordinates a workflow and only runs "targets" or steps in your workflow that require updating due to downstream changes, saving costly runtime and ensuring that everything is updated when changes occur. This results in more trustworthy outputs and reproducible computation. The session will run as follows:

* Introduction presentation (20-30 mins)
* Short demo (15-20 mins)
* Application (completed individually, in break out rooms, or all together, depending on size of group) (20-30 mins)

**This event will be** **virtual. Please RSVP to receive a Zoom link on the day of the event.**

This workshop will be interactive. To follow along with Tracey's demo and participate in the interactive portion of the workshop, **please fork and clone the following GitHub repository:** [https://github.com/traceybit/rladies-sb-targets](http://clicks.meetup.com/ls/click?upn=XbaZ37larFA-2FuV5MohrYpXag6tzzSKkPkolS2igSlDFbaBDj9506S0APr-2Fk5r-2BnN5DCsh3C7GWZGA9KT-2BuKdvw-3D-3DveVI_uAGIKxVHnzoGVmPcnUwHmR2TNcd1XwKmxiXrbTcDN7iQ2ay5Epo0Q7VJrJTk6vDREdfKvexzTrcowizgD8wSEXny81zzRpmb1yNLjzfDTT9H2ZUh1HgvXFKSavtUYquW34R6GUNDo5vUa1dqKGzmWMdzuu-2Fesx-2FUA4v9j5LZn9J4zsW-2BTdOjkGmiMwOuxtyjxupFqSstOgIagT6t6ij3LDrhniahRIW-2F4dNjg8yYL9pZ-2Fs7YjvEShFZjmUQZYZHJEl2nhJxsLaFknduVsNyus1wm-2FNzOsgkWtZtiXLOvbLxBja9CsHPX-2FfdfcFOEqYdSXWpL8kQPwXktRa6kR2JuZaqk-2FNLXRLlfH6ok5U3PSPCAQa-2BpjrwbvEdRdthbXuDlIovfZD9vDXt5GPDOy-2F8ex14konGP2ZLiWPiXeuG3WVHVIVxsUcTjkuvWTBbalWKawRSXs-2F8aAi-2BBxhOQHBePJNKIycCPedud-2BDjUmDBP94ZbyWr5fa1oyZkABY8DJnphGEOKO89D7DZO0QrgKDg36m-2FDHrbsrrUBqXv-2FMxjE4O7sMjka9Avv6zHefRHEHrMuP4c8XqroTP-2BzvVkaUlV0ue5QHkQODqQrj82cMkHwGfMxhfXPMnP9-2BBJMQ0bjN5ZLNidBqxrvjwuys9p2grnuEN77B-2BK5RmBIseKmbQulq08-3D). If you need assistance with this step, please reach out!

We will be using the following packages: tidyverse, here, and, of course, targets. You can install packages using the following code: install.packages("insert_package_name").

**Important Meetup information:**
To ensure a safe online environment for all, we ask that attendees are aware of the following:

1. RSVPs will close on Wednesday 10/11 at 12:00pm PT. **Please be sure to RSVP before then to ensure admission.**
2. We will send out the meeting Zoom link and passcode to those on the RSVP list on Wednesday 10/11 at 4:00pm PT. **Please be sure to check your email for this information! *The Zoom link and passcode will not be shared publicly on the Meetup page.***

title: Introdução ao R e Aplicações
description:
Heey, tudo bem?

Estamos muito felizes com a sua participação.

Aproveite o curso. Aproveite essa oportunidade! Este evento foi pensado com muito carinho para você.

O que você vai aprender?

**O curso abordará conceitos fundamentais da análise de dados utilizando a linguagem de programação R, com foco na manipulação de dados com o pacote dplyr e na criação de visualizações com o pacote ggplot2.**

Vem brilhar com a R-Ladies Goiânia?

Quais pacotes vão ser utilizados ?

* library(tidyverse)

**Sobre o Projeto CasaNem**

O projeto **CasaNem** é um centro de acolhimento da cidade do Rio de Janeiro que abriga pessoas LGBTQI+ em situação de vulnerabilidade social, em sua maioria pessoas transexuais e travestis, e que desenvolve programas e atividades em diversas vertentes com atividades focadas na autonomia e cultura dos seus moradores, além de realizar atendimentos e oferecer cursos e oficinas. Em 2020, a CasaNem atingia direta e indiretamente mais de 100 pessoas.

Quer conhecer um pouco mais esse projeto? Clica aí [https://evoe.cc/casanem](https://evoe.cc/casanem)

**E aí? Topa?**

Link de inscrição: https://www.sympla.com.br/produtor/cursosonline

Dúvidas frequentes:

CERTIFICADOS! - O certificado de 4h do evento será enviado para o seu e-mail. Caso você não receba ao final do evento, escreva para goiania@rladies.org.

MATERIAIS! - Os materiais utilizados no nosso curso (apresentação, scripts) serão disponibilizados através do nosso GitHub .

Dicas importantes para melhor aproveitamento do evento

(1°) Procure conectar o seu computador no cabo de rede, evitando instabilidades na transmissão;
(2°) Pré-configure a sua câmera e microfone antes de entrar no evento pois será útil para as interações com a professora em caso de dúvidas!
(3°) Prepare seu ambiente, aquela caneca de café, garrafa de água e os fones de ouvido, melhores companheiros para se concentrar e mergulhar de cabeça nessa experiência!
(4°) Não guarde esse conhecimento apenas para você, compartilhe com seus amigos e redes sociais marcando a gente no instagram @rladiesgyn.

Você faz parte desse compromisso

Lembre-se que o Código de Conduta também se estende à versão online de nossos eventos e não será tolerada qualquer forma de assédios ou intimidações de qualquer participante, seja audiência, palestrantes, colaboradores, ou quaisquer membros da comunidade.

A gente brilha TOGETHER!

title: R beginner workshop series - Part 4!
description:
SAVE THE DATE!

RLadies Vancouver will host a 4-week Intro to R workshop series on manipulating, analyzing, and plotting data in R.

This workshop is intended for anyone who:

* has no prior coding experience in R or other languages.

* has little self-taught coding experience.

* is proficient in other languages, such as Python, and would like to transition into R.

More details will be posted here on the content and instructors.

Please RSVP to each individual session so we know how many people to expect. We highly recommend everyone attend all sessions. If you cannot make it one week or just need a refresher on specific topics, we will make the materials available after each session.

See you there!

Yuka, Jasmine, Melissa and Cici

**IMPORTANT**\*\*

1. Have your computer and a steady internet connection ready

2. If this is your first R-Ladies event, please take a moment to review our R-Ladies Global code of conduct: https://github.com/rladies/.github/blob/master/CODE_OF_CONDUCT.md

3. I don't identify as one of the "ladies", but can I attend? Of course! Everyone is welcome to join us at our events and to be a part of our mission to empower gender minorities and underrepresented people in data science with the skills and knowledge to code in one of the top programming languages in data science, which is R.

To be a part of our community, we ask participants to be actively respectful, encouraging, and supportive of each other.

title: Watch Party Francophone pour la posit::conf(2023)
description:
**En collaboration avec R-Ladies Paris je vous présentes le Posit Watch Party!**
**Nous sommes ravis d'organiser une Watch Party virtuelle pour la Conférence Posit qui va avoir lieu du 17 au 20 septembre 2023 à Chicago 🔗 ​[https://posit.co/conference/ ](https://posit.co/conference/)**

**Durant cette Watch Party, nous regarderons les replays de ces deux présentations sélectionnées de la Conférence Posit, le mardi 3 octobre 2023 à partir de 19h00 CEST :**

1. **GitHub Copilot integration with RStudio, it's finally here!, présenté par**

**Tom Mock, Product Manager, Posit Workbench and RStudio, Posit, PBC**

**“ This talk closes issue #10148, "Github Copilot integration with RStudio", the most upvoted feature request in RStudio's history. Code-generating AI tools like Github Copilot™ promise an "AI pair programmer that offers autocomplete-style suggestions as you code". For the first time, we'll show a native integration of Copilot into RStudio, helping to build on that promise by providing AI-generated "ghost text" autocompletion with R and other languages. I'll also provide a comparison of Copilot's "ghost text" to a chat-style interface in RStudio via the {chattr} package from the Posit MLVerse team. To make the most of these new features, I'll share some examples of how sharing additional context, comments, code, and other "prompt engineering" can help you go from code-generating AI tools that feel like an annoying backseat driver to an experienced copilot. We'll close with a robust end-to-end example of how these new RStudio integrations and packages can help you be a more productive developer.”**

2. **Unlock the Power of DataViz Animation and Interactivity in Quarto, présenté par**

**Deepsha Menghani, Sr. Data Applied Scientist Manager, Microsoft**
**“Data visualizations are a key tool in the process of sharing insights with stakeholders in ways that make an impact and are easily understood. Animation and interactivity bring in a key component of analysis that enables one to derive insights that a plot with two dimensions doesn't otherwise allow. Sometimes it can tell a powerful story, and sometimes it can just add some flair to an otherwise low-key story. This talk will showcase packages such as Plotly and Crosstalk that enable this creativity right within Quarto.**
**Plotly, Crosstalk, and Highcharter are powerful packages that allow interactivity right from within your Quarto markdown report, without the need to create an entire application. Easily have two separate plots interact with each other or add drill-down capabilities within a single plot, whichever adds to the story you are trying to tell.”**

**Mouna Belaid, de R-Ladies Paris, partagera également son expérience personnelle de sa participation à la conférence et ses impressions. Ensuite, nous présenterons chaque talk et tiendrons une discussion ouverte après chaque talk.**

**N'hésitez pas à partager ce meetup avec toute personne susceptible d'être intéressée.**

**Ne manquez pas ce privilège d'être parmi les premiers à avoir accès aux replays de très haute qualité.**

**Nous vous attendons nombreux à cette première Watch Party francophone ! ✨**

title: Watch Party Francophone pour la posit::conf(2023)
description:
Nous sommes ravis de collaborer avec R-Ladies Strasbourg pour organiser une Watch Party virtuelle pour la conférence organisée par Posit qui a lieu du 17 au 20 septembre 2023 à Chicago 🔗 ​[https://posit.co/conference/](https://posit.co/conference/)

Durant cette Watch Party, nous regarderons ensemble les replays de ces deux présentations sélectionnées de la conférence, le mardi 3 octobre 2023 à partir de 19h00 CEST :

1. **GitHub Copilot integration with RStudio, it's finally here!**, présenté par
Tom Mock, Product Manager, Posit Workbench and RStudio, Posit, PBC

“ This talk closes issue #10148, "Github Copilot integration with RStudio", the most upvoted feature request in RStudio's history. Code-generating AI tools like Github Copilot™ promise an "AI pair programmer that offers autocomplete-style suggestions as you code". For the first time, we'll show a native integration of Copilot into RStudio, helping to build on that promise by providing AI-generated "ghost text" autocompletion with R and other languages. I'll also provide a comparison of Copilot's "ghost text" to a chat-style interface in RStudio via the {chattr} package from the Posit MLVerse team. To make the most of these new features, I'll share some examples of how sharing additional context, comments, code, and other "prompt engineering" can help you go from code-generating AI tools that feel like an annoying backseat driver to an experienced copilot. We'll close with a robust end-to-end example of how these new RStudio integrations and packages can help you be a more productive developer.”

2. **Unlock the Power of DataViz Animation and Interactivity in Quarto**, présenté par Deepsha Menghani, Sr. Data Applied Scientist Manager, Microsoft

“Data visualizations are a key tool in the process of sharing insights with stakeholders in ways that make an impact and are easily understood. Animation and interactivity bring in a key component of analysis that enables one to derive insights that a plot with two dimensions doesn't otherwise allow. Sometimes it can tell a powerful story, and sometimes it can just add some flair to an otherwise low-key story. This talk will showcase packages such as Plotly and Crosstalk that enable this creativity right within Quarto.
Plotly, Crosstalk, and Highcharter are powerful packages that allow interactivity right from within your Quarto markdown report, without the need to create an entire application. Easily have two separate plots interact with each other or add drill-down capabilities within a single plot, whichever adds to the story you are trying to tell.”

Mouna Belaid partagera également son expérience personnelle de sa participation à la conférence et ses impressions. Après la présentation de chaque talk, nous tiendrons une discussion ouverte.

N'hésitez pas à partager ce meetup avec toute personne susceptible d'être intéressée.

Ne manquez pas ce privilège d'être parmi les premiers à avoir accès à des replays de très haute qualité.

Nous vous attendons nombreux à cette première Watch Party francophone ! ✨

title: Hello, Quarto: A World of Possibilities (for Reproducible Publishing)
description:
R-Ladies Bergen and R-Ladies Cologne are kicking off a series on "**Toolbox for Reproducibility**" where we will dive into tips, tricks, and techniques on how to adhere to best standards and make our code (and workflows) reproducible.

We’re more than excited to have Mine Çetinkaya-Rundel, professor at Duke University (USA) as well as Developer Educator at Posit, as our first speaker and learn from her how to use Quarto to discover the world of possibilities.

**✨ Hello, Quarto: A World of Possibilities (for Reproducible Publishing) ✨**

Quarto is an open-source scientific and technical publishing system that unifies and extends the R Markdown ecosystem. This talk will introduce you to creating reproducible, computational documents, from course notes to journal manuscripts to books and web pages with Quarto. Familiarity with R and RStudio will be helpful for following along with the demos, however, those who work with any computational documents in any language (e.g., Jupyter Notebooks with Python) will also find the content relevant, particularly since Quarto is language-agnostic and can be used to create dynamic content with any computing language.

If you’re up for joining us, RSVP here. We will share all access details for our virtual event beforehand.

To stay up-to-date with our future events, make sure to also follow us online ([R-Ladies Bergen](https://www.meetup.com/rladies-bergen/) and [@rladies_cologne](https://mas.to/@rladies_cologne@fosstodon.org)) 👩‍💻

We’re very much looking forward to seeing many of you! ☺️

title: GRUPO 3. TALLER DE ESTUDIO LIBRO "R PARA CIENCIA DE DATOS"
description:
¡Desde RLadies Bariloche les invitamos a comenzar con el estudio en grupo del libro R para Ciencia de Datos (versión traducida al español de R for Data Science) de Hadley Wickham (el creador de Tidyverse y varios paquetes más!). El libro es de libre acceso en https://es.r4ds.hadley.nz/ ¡Está muy bueno!

• Nos juntaremos de forma virtual una vez cada 15 días para avanzar con la lectura y comprensión del libro y los scripts. Cada encuentro será guiado por uno o dos integrantes del grupo.

• En el primer encuentro discutiremos el capitulo número 3 “visualización de datos” (¡Qué bueno, empezamos con gráficos con ggplot2 ) https://es.r4ds.hadley.nz/visualizaci%C3%B3n-de-datos.html Es necesario llegar a la reunión con el capítulo previamente leído y trabajado. Quienes no leyeron la introducción del libro aún: adelante, es corta, y les dará un panorama de lo que iremos estudiando. En la reunión de organización vimos un panorama de la introducción.

• En base a la encuesta de horarios que hicimos, hemos definido tres grupos de trabajo con distintos horarios. ¡ATENCIÓN! ¡Por favor, anotarse solo en uno de los tres! AHORA ESTÁS EN EL MEETUP DEL GRUPO 3, DE JUEVES DE 18 A 20.

• ¡Importante! Ni bien se inscriban en el meetup, entren a los ajustes de su perfil y verifiquen tener activas las notificaciones, no sólo del meetup en general, sino del grupo RLadies-BRC en particular (figuran abajo del todo en notificaciones por correo electrónico) para poder recibir luego el enlace para conectarse y otras notificaciones relacionadas al evento.

• Quienes se inscriban al meetup y si aún no se sumaron, van a recibir más adelante un enlace para sumarse al espacio de trabajo de slack que es exclusivo para el estudio del libro y que será nuestra principal vía de comunicación. Si nunca lo usaron y/o necesitan ayuda para familiarizarse, escríbannos y les damos una orientación rápida.

• Necesitamos uno o dos voluntarias o voluntarios para guiar el capítulo número 3. Interesadxs nos lo pueden comunicar por el slack o al correo bariloche@rladies.org. Cuanto antes, mejor. En caso de que nadie se ofrezca, haremos un sorteo (con el comando sample() en R!!!).

• A partir del primer encuentro, cada grupo de trabajo se auto-gestionará. Por ejemplo, sorteará o decidirá quién guiará el siguiente capítulo, si verán un capítulo, medio capítulo o dos capítulos (la propuesta general es ir despacio, lento pero sin tregua), si aplicarán lo que se vió en el capítulo a otro set de datos etc. Contamos con el espacio de slack para consultarnos y compartir información en cada grupo y entre grupos.

Al anotarse, les pedimos que primero lean y acepten el código de conducta que encontrarán en la página.

Para asistir al meetup, les recomendamos poseer una cuenta de google (utilizaremos google meet). Se recomienda tener conocimientos básicos de R.

¡ATENCIÓN! por cuestiones de seguridad virtual, el enlace a la reunión se enviará unos minutos antes de la misma. Por ello les pedimos que
estén alertas al correo.

title: R beginner workshop series - Part 3!
description:
SAVE THE DATE!

RLadies Vancouver will host a 4-week Intro to R workshop series on manipulating, analyzing, and plotting data in R.

This workshop is intended for anyone who:

* has no prior coding experience in R or other languages.

* has little self-taught coding experience.

* is proficient in other languages, such as Python, and would like to transition into R.

More details will be posted here on the content and instructors.

Please RSVP to each individual session so we know how many people to expect. We highly recommend everyone attend all sessions. If you cannot make it one week or just need a refresher on specific topics, we will make the materials available after each session.

See you there!

Yuka, Jasmine, Melissa and Cici

**IMPORTANT**\*\*

1. Have your computer and a steady internet connection ready

2. If this is your first R-Ladies event, please take a moment to review our R-Ladies Global code of conduct: https://github.com/rladies/.github/blob/master/CODE_OF_CONDUCT.md

3. I don't identify as one of the "ladies", but can I attend? Of course! Everyone is welcome to join us at our events and to be a part of our mission to empower gender minorities and underrepresented people in data science with the skills and knowledge to code in one of the top programming languages in data science, which is R.

To be a part of our community, we ask participants to be actively respectful, encouraging, and supportive of each other.
