title: Estructuras condicionales en R
description:
\*\* Descripción
Este taller te permitirá aprender a manejar las estructuras de control en R, un aspecto fundamental en la programación para automatizar tareas repetitivas o condicionales. A lo largo de dos horas, veremos cómo utilizar las estructuras de control más comunes en R, como el bucle for, el bucle while y las estructuras if-else. Aprenderás a escribir código eficiente y legible, evitando la repetición innecesaria de código y mejorando la legibilidad de tus programas. El taller está dirigido a personas con conocimientos básicos de R. Al finalizar el taller, los participantes tendrán las habilidades necesarias para aplicar estructuras de control en R a sus propios proyectos y análisis de datos.

\*\* De la ponente
La Dra. Mirna Vazquez Rosas Landa es investigadora en el Instituto de Ciencias de Mar y Limnología de la UNAM, donde se dedica a estudiar la vida microbiana del océano. Su trabajo se enfoca en explorar preguntas sobre biogeografía, evolución, ecología y fisiología de los microorganismos. Utiliza enfoques computacionales y experimentales para responder a estas preguntas y su interés principal es entender cuáles son los factores que determinan la distribución de las especies microbianas en el océano, entre otros. Además de su trabajo en la investigación, la Dra. Vazquez tiene una pasión por enseñar y compartir conocimientos. Es co-organizadora de RLadies Xalapa desde el 2018 y es una instructora activa en la Comunidad de Desarrolladores de Software en Bioinformática y en la Red Mexicana de Bioinformática.

title: Introduction to Bayesian GLMs with brms
description:
**SPEAKER**
This online workshop will be led by Dr Bodo Winter (https://bodowinter.com/)(@BodoWinter). Bodo is a Senior Lecturer at the University of Birmingham, UK, and the author of the leading R book "Statistics for Linguists: An Introduction Using R". (I Highly recommend getting one🤩.). We are thrilled to have Bodo again!!🥳🥳👏👏

**WORKSHOP OVERVIEW**
For this workshop session, Bodo intends to walk people through the core insights of the generalized linear model (GLM) framework, with an emphasis on Poisson regression and logistic regression, two approaches that are incredibly useful when analyzing discrete data. We will work on two examples of experiments involving co-speech gestures and fit both types of GLMs.

**PREPARATION**
Knitted markdowns will be provided, but attendees who want to run the models themselves should install the packages “tidyverse,” “brms” and “tidybayes” packages. Unfortunately, brms installation can sometimes be tricky. Please attend even if you don’t get it running, as you’ll still be able to benefit!

**ONLINE PROGRAMME**
The workshop will be held online, starting on 11th May at 16:00 (UK time). Please join us via this Zoom link:
[https://bham-ac-uk.zoom.us/j/87984238119?pwd=VDBLSGFsckJBbC9YT08xY05kazZMUT09](https://bham-ac-uk.zoom.us/j/87984238119?pwd=VDBLSGFsckJBbC9YT08xY05kazZMUT09)

**CODE OF CONDUCT**
Please take a minute to read our code of conduct - we are committed to creating a safe and harassment-free environment:
https://github.com/rladies/.github/blob/master/CODE_OF_CONDUCT.md
R-Ladies events are always free and open to anyone who wants to participate as long as they follow the code of conduct. We are pro-actively inclusive of queer, trans, and all minority identities, with additional sensitivity to intersectional identities. Our priority is to provide a safe community space for anyone identifying as a minority gender who is interested in and/or working with R.

title: Exploratory data analysis and visualisation
description:
Exploratory data analysis (EDA) is a critical (and sometimes under-appreciated!) step that precedes statistical analysis. This includes steps like calculating descriptive statistics, finding extreme values, and summarizing the data -- in general, getting familiar with your raw data before you start to apply a statistical model! In this workshop, we will use dplyr (tidyverse) commands, like group_by() and summarize(), to highlight these important EDA steps.

EDA also includes graphing raw data to assess initial trends. The workshop will also cover different kinds of data visualisations, including box plots, scatterplots with trend lines, and bar plots with the ggplot package. The focus will be on quickly deploying informative graphs and selecting the right geom()s for the data types you have.

No previous knowledge is assumed, just general experience with R (loading packages, reading in data, etc.). If you'd like to polish up your tidyverse skills before joining, check out our previous workshop on data wrangling: https://youtu.be/NRgdTaAfwTo

title: Dernière Exploration du {tidyverse} avec le Package {purrr}
description:
R-Ladies Paris vous donne rendez-vous le mardi 2 mai à partir de 14H en direct sur Twitch. Notre invité Marie Vaugoyeau va parler d'itération avec le package {purrr}.

Au programme (non définitif) :

➿ Qu'est-ce que c'est l'itération et comment la traiter avec {purrr}
🔤 Présentation d'un cas simple avec du texte
🗂️ Application de fonctions sur différents jeu de données
📈 Réalisation de graphiques répétés

Vous pouvez retrouver les derniers directs de Marie sur Twitch ici https://www.twitch.tv/marievaugoyeau/videos où elle a abordé les sujets suivants :

✔️ Temps fort : Le {tidyverse} c'est quoi ? Mise en lumière des packages {dplyr} et {tidyr}
✔️ Temps fort : Suite de l'exploration du {tidyverse} avec la mise en avant des packages {forcats}, {stringr} et {purrr}

➡️ A propos de notre invité :

Marie Vaugoyeau (https://mvaugoyeau.netlify.com/) est accompagnatrice indépendante à l'analyse de données et la formation au langage R. En plus de ses missions de consulting, elle diffuse ses savoirs en R sur plusieurs plateformes. Rejoignez Marie tous les mois dans un live sur Twitch ! Elle rédige une newsletter de manière irrégulière pour parler de ses inspirations et des trucs et astuces sur R. Vous pouvez vous inscrire à la newsletter ici 👉 https://tinyurl.com/2a7uwhnr Elle est également l'auteure du 📘 livre Langage R et statistiques 🔗 https://www.editions-eni.fr/livre/langage-r-et-statistiques-initiation-a-l-analyse-de-donnees-9782409036934 Que vous soyez débutant, novice ou junior dans la science des données, ce livre est là pour vous accompagner dans l’acquisition des connaissances indispensables pour la réalisation de statistiques et l’analyse de tous types de données avec le langage R. 📺 Vous pouvez aussi retrouver Marie sur sa chaîne YouTube https://www.youtube.com/@vaugoyeaumarie3978
L'inscription est désormais disponible sur notre groupe Meetup.

Pour ceux qui se sont inscrits à notre meetup, nous allons bientôt partager avec vous le lien Twitch afin d'accèder au live.

N'hésitez pas à créer un compte sur Twitch https://www.twitch.tv/ si vous souhaitez poser des questions à Marie dans le chat.

Si vous souhaitez revoir nos précédents meetups, les enregistrements sont disponibles sur notre chaine YouTube 📺 https://www.youtube.com/@rladiesparis/videos

Nous vous attendons nombreux !

title: Introdução ao pacote purrr
description:
Heey, tudo bem?

Estamos muito felizes com a sua participação.

Aproveite o curso. Aproveite essa oportunidade! Este evento foi pensado com muito carinho para você.

O que você vai aprender?

O pacote purrr aprimora o kit de ferramentas de programação funcional (FP) do R, fornecendo um conjunto completo e consistente de ferramentas para trabalhar com funções e vetores. Você irá aprender a dar seus primeiros passos com o pacote purrr.

Vem brilhar com a R-Ladies Goiânia?

Quais pacotes vão ser utilizados ?

* library(tidyverse)
* library(purrr)

**Sobre o Projeto CasaNem**

O projeto **CasaNem** é um centro de acolhimento da cidade do Rio de Janeiro que abriga pessoas LGBTQI+ em situação de vulnerabilidade social, em sua maioria pessoas transexuais e travestis, e que desenvolve programas e atividades em diversas vertentes com atividades focadas na autonomia e cultura dos seus moradores, além de realizar atendimentos e oferecer cursos e oficinas. Em 2020, a CasaNem atingia direta e indiretamente mais de 100 pessoas.

Quer conhecer um pouco mais esse projeto? Clica aí [https://evoe.cc/casanem](https://evoe.cc/casanem)

**E aí? Topa?**

Link de inscrição: https://www.sympla.com.br/produtor/cursosonline

Dúvidas frequentes:

CERTIFICADOS! - O certificado de 4h do evento será enviado para o seu e-mail. Caso você não receba ao final do evento, escreva para goiania@rladies.org.

MATERIAIS! - Os materiais utilizados no nosso curso (apresentação, scripts) serão disponibilizados através do nosso GitHub .

Dicas importantes para melhor aproveitamento do evento

(1°) Procure conectar o seu computador no cabo de rede, evitando instabilidades na transmissão;
(2°) Pré-configure a sua câmera e microfone antes de entrar no evento pois será útil para as interações com a professora em caso de dúvidas!
(3°) Prepare seu ambiente, aquela caneca de café, garrafa de água e os fones de ouvido, melhores companheiros para se concentrar e mergulhar de cabeça nessa experiência!
(4°) Não guarde esse conhecimento apenas para você, compartilhe com seus amigos e redes sociais marcando a gente no instagram @rladiesgyn.

Você faz parte desse compromisso

Lembre-se que o Código de Conduta também se estende à versão online de nossos eventos e não será tolerada qualquer forma de assédios ou intimidações de qualquer participante, seja audiência, palestrantes, colaboradores, ou quaisquer membros da comunidade.

A gente brilha TOGETHER!

title: 4° Meetup Virtual R-Ladies Salvador
description:
Olá, pessoal! 🌐💜

Em abril teremos um minicurso: “Relatórios reprodutíveis com Rmarkdown e pagedreport” .

Com Camila Braz Soares, dando início às nossas trilhas formativas de 2023.

Aprenda a fazer lindos relatórios reprodutíveis utilizando R Markdown. Use uma interface de notebook produtiva para combinar texto, fórmulas e código para produzir relatórios elegantes.

O pagedreport é um pacote R para ajudá-las a criar relatórios em PDF a partir de belos templates utilizando RMarkdown.

Os principais tópicos deste curso incluem:
▶️ A linguagem markdown
▶️ Relatórios com RMarkdown
▶️ Templates de relatórios utilizando o pacote pagedreport

Camila é formada em Estatística pela UFBA, mestranda em Matemática com área de concentração em Estatística na UFBA e é analista de dados da SEI Bahia - Superintendência de Estudos Econômicos e Sociais da Bahia.

Já anote na sua agenda, dia 29/04 das 09:00 às 12:00 e faça sua inscrição no link a seguir: **[Link Sympla ](https://www.sympla.com.br/evento-online/relatorios-reprodutiveis-com-rmarkdown-e-pagedreport/1927338?_gl=1*lmv2e2*_ga*MTY5MTcyMzExLjE2Nzk1MTk1MDQ.*_ga_KXH10SQTZF*MTY3OTUxOTUwNC4xLjEuMTY3OTUyMTYzNS4wLjAuMA..)**😉

title: Introducción a Tidymodels. Parte 2: modelos mixtos
description:
¡Hola!

Esta segunda parte del taller de introducción a Tidymodels estará a cargo de Alejandra Tapia Silva y estará dirigida a personas nuevas en el modelado con efectos mixtos. Cubrirá aspectos introductorios de este tema esperando que sea un punto de partida para el uso de estos modelos con R y {tidymodels}. No se utilizan fórmulas matemáticas, en un intento de hacerlo amigable e intuitivo y así poder dar atención a una audiencia más amplia.

Para aprovechar mejor este taller, es necesario tener algo de experiencia con análisis exploratorio de datos y modelos lineales, de tal manera de poder transitar sin mucho obstáculo teórico por cada una de las etapas del modelamiento con efectos mixtos. Además, es necesario contar con conocimientos iniciales de algunos paquetes del tidyverse (como {readr} y {dplyr}).

REQUERIMIENTOS

Además de {tidyverse} y {tidymodels}, que utilizamos en la primera parte de este taller, es necesario instalar los siguientes paquetes:
\> install\.packages\(c\("broom\.mixed"\,"lme4"\,"lmerTest"\)\)
Luego del taller se dejarán referencias para quienes tengan interés en una explicación más detallada sobre modelos mixtos y {tidymodels}, de tal manera que cuando comiencen a usar estas herramientas con sus propios datos dispongan de mayor información.

CÓDIGO DE CONDUCTA

Queremos que este sea un espacio de aprendizaje amable y seguro. Al inscribirte en este evento estás aceptando nuestro código de conducta: https://guide.rladies.org/about/coc/#spanish

¡Te esperamos!

R-Ladies

title: Switching between space and time: Spatio-temporal analysis with cubble
description:
Dear all,

Our next meetup will take place **live at TU Wien**. Our speaker is **Sherry Zhang** who is visiting from Australia and will introduce us to the **cubble** package, walking us through how we can work with spatial data and temporal data in R, and how we can more easily combine the two using *cubble*.

**Abstract**: Weather data have a spatial component given by the station coordinates and a temporal component that provides measurements on precipitation, maximum and minimum temperature. This will be a hand-on session to work with weather station data. We will use the package *sf* to work on the spatial side to generate some maps and the package *tsibble* to conduct some exploratory data analysis on the temporal side. One way to communicate space and time information together is through a glyph map where temporal coordinates are transformed into space. We will create the glyph map with the *ggplot* geom: *geom_glyph()*. Lastly, we will experiment with a new data structure, *cubble*, to arrange spatio-temporal data.

Best regards,
Your R-Ladies Vienna Organizing Team

title: TidyTuesday with R-Ladies Philly
description:
[Tidy Tuesday](https://github.com/rfordatascience/tidytuesday) is a weekly data project where R enthusiasts across the globe share their plots, analysis, and thoughts about a new data set.
It's a great way to get started with R or really stretch your R skills in a fun way and try something new.

At this interactive event, we will facilitate some analysis and live coding of this week’s Tidy Tuesday dataset guided by what attendees want to try!

This event is welcome to all. If you are completely new to R, we won’t be covering installation, set-up, or other introductory topics. However, it should still be a fun event to join and learn by watching.

**This is an online event co-hosted by R-Ladies DC and R-Ladies Philly. Please feel free to RSVP on one or both meetup pages.**

description:
**[Agenda]**
• 19:30 \~ 19:40 (10 mins) 開場

**BYOC：打造自己的 ChatGPT－大型語言模型分散式訓練分享**

**[Talk Information]**
整理並分享 PyTorch 分散式訓練、大型語言模型節省 GPU 資源的方法
\- 知道有分散式訓練這個技巧
\- 知道怎麼在多顆 GPU 的單台機器上啟動分散式訓練

title: Modeling infectious diseases
description:
**R-Ladies NYC & R-Ladies Rome** are coming together for a joint event.

Get ready for an electrifying presentation!

In this talk, we will delve into the fascinating world of modeling diseases with R using both deterministic and Bayesian SIR model methods while exploring the exciting insights that each field has to offer.

At the heart of our discussion is the well-known Sir model, this model can help us understand how an epidemic might unfold and what measures can be taken to prevent its spread.

So, do not miss this fascinating talk about modeling infectious diseases. Whether you are a student of science, a healthcare professional, or simply someone who wants to understand more about how to use R for modeling diseases, this talk promises to be insightful!

**Schedule of the event:**

* Introduction presentation of the Chapters and [R-Ladies Global](https://www.linkedin.com/notifications/?filter=all#) action
* SIR model with R - quick intro assessment (Speaker [Federica Gazzelloni](https://www.linkedin.com/notifications/?filter=all#))
* Bayesian workflow for disease transmission (Speaker [Jacqueline Novik](https://www.linkedin.com/notifications/?filter=all#))
* Q&A session

\-\-\-\-
Si parla di malattie infettive. In questo talk, approfondiremo l'affascinante mondo delle malattie. Utilizzeremo R per il modello di tipo deterministico e bayesiano, e esploreremo le entusiasmanti intuizioni che ogni campo ha da offrire.

Al centro della nostra discussione c'è il noto modello Sir, questo modello può aiutarci a capire come potrebbe evolve un'epidemia, discuteremo su possibili modi di calibrare i modelli, e quali misure possono essere prese per prevenirne la diffusione.

Quindi, non perdere questo meetup! Che tu sia uno studente di scienze, un professionista sanitario o semplicemente qualcuno che vuole capire di più su come usare R per applicare i modelli per le malattie infettive.

title: Crea tu portafolio web para búsquedas de empleo con Quarto y RStudio
description:
**Crea tu portafolio web para búsquedas de empleo y servicios freelance con Quarto y RStudio**

**Dictado por: Jess Garriga y Jan Strappa**

**¿De qué trata el taller?**

Un portafolio es una colección de proyectos y trabajos destacados que muestran a posibles clientes o empleadores tus habilidades y experiencia. Con este taller, adquirirás los conocimientos necesarios para crear y publicar un elegante portafolio web que podrás personalizar según tus necesidades. Aprenderás a utilizar Quarto para conseguir un sitio web de aspecto profesional de forma gratuita y sin tener que preocuparte demasiado por detalles técnicos. El taller está pensado para cualquier persona que quiera hacer un portafolio web atractivo para mostrar su experiencia laboral, habilidades, y/o promover sus servicios. Está dirigida a principiantes en adelante que quieran beneficiarse de herramientas de código abierto, gratuitas, libres, fáciles de usar y mantener, y sin requerir alojamiento de pago. Será útil estar familiarizade con lenguajes de marcado y con la interfaz de RStudio.

**Inscripción**

* Únete a este evento en meetup para mantenerte en contacto con las novedades.
* Como este evento se realiza en el marco del FLISOL Valle de Uco, hay que completar el siguiente formulario: [https://forms.gle/QKUzdMNTxgy1aG2ZA](ESTE FORMULARIO )

title: R-Ladies @ BSC |  Tools for Climate Forecasting & AI for Cancer Immunotherapy
description:
Join us for an amazing workshop, co-organized with BarcelonaR, at the Barcelona Supercomputing Centre!

**Programme**
*18:30* -> Introduction of R-Ladies Barcelona and BarcelonaR user group.
18:40 -> "Collaborative R tools for Climate Forecast Analysis by HPC" (An-Chi Ho and Eva Rifà)
19:00 -> "AI in R: PredIG an explainable XGBoost predictor for cancer immunotherapy" (Roc Farriol-Duran)

**"Collaborative R tools for Climate Forecast Analysis by HPC"** (An-Chi and Eva)
A large amount of data is used in the study of climate. This is why the use of HPC is necessary to carry out the analysis and processing of climate data. In addition, multidisciplinary workflows require the use of shared tools that originate collaboration between scientists and engineers. The talk presents some R packages developed within the Computational Earth Sciences group of the Barcelona Supercomputing Center (BSC-CNS). It also highlights how collaboration between climate scientists and data engineers using R tools can improve our understanding of climate patterns and lead to better predictions. The use of HPC can speed up calculations and enable the handling of large data sets, making it a valuable tool in climate analysis and prediction.

**"AI in R: PredIG an explainable XGBoost predictor for cancer immunotherapy"** (Roc)
AI in R is (of course) possible. XGBoost is a state-of-the-art algorithm (Kaggle winner, specially working on tabular data) based on gradient boosting and decision trees that is fully implemented in R (R-package: “xgboost”). Here, we will show an implementation on a biomedical research question: how to predict the immune response against a tumor (and its mutations)?. This case will allow us to focus on a binary classification problem with extreme class imbalance (due to the nature of real patient data) and how (re)tuning XGBoost to deal with imbalance can make it work.

*Language*: English

*Requirements*: There are no specific requirements.

venues.name: Barcelona Supercomputing Center - Centre Nacional de Supercomputació

title: Plotting with R
description:
We are finally having Rladies meetups in person again! For this session we will be creating Tidy Tuesday plots together (https://github.com/rfordatascience/tidytuesday). Tidy Tuesday is an ongoing project by the R4DS Online Learning Community where a new dataset is posted each week aimed at helping learners practice their wrangling and visualization skills.

Don’t know where to start? During the start of the meeting, we will have a short tutorial and write some code together to get your plots started. We also included some resources below to help provide some inspiration!

Come out and meet some R-Ladies from Vancouver and try out some new data visualization skills with us!

Workshop details:
Date and time: April 20 2023, doors at 5:45pm, 6:00pm start

**IMPORTANT**\*\*

1. Please put your full name and confirm that you are over the age of 18 when you sign up. This is a requirement of the venue and we will need to provide that information to them to get access to the appropriate floor.
2. Bring your computer and charger (if needed)
3. If this is your first R-Ladies event, please take a moment to review our R-Ladies Global code of conduct: https://github.com/rladies/.github/blob/master/CODE_OF_CONDUCT.md
4. Who is allowed to attend the workshop?
Everyone is welcome to join us at our events and to be a part of our mission to empower gender minorities with the skills and knowledge to code in one of the top programming languages in data science, which is R.

To fulfill this mission we do ask participants to respect a focus on the encouragement and support for women and non-binary people during our events.

***

General workshop information:

-Please expect a hands-on session in a friendly and laid-back atmosphere where we are all encouraged to learn together.

Software requirements:

* R (Free at https://cran.r-project.org/)
* RStudio (Free at https://www.rstudio.com/products/rstudio/download/)

Resources:
Data Visualization https://clauswilke.com/dataviz/
Wrangle your data with dplyr - https://allisonhorst.shinyapps.io/dplyr-learnr/

Colour Palettes:
Wes Anderson Colour Palette https://github.com/karthik/wesanderson
Studio Ghibli Colour Palette https://ewenme.github.io/ghibli/
Calecopal (inspired by different California ecosystems and created by An Bui!) https://github.com/an-bui/calecopal
Sci-Fi Themed Colour Palette https://cran.r-project.org/web/packages/ggsci/vignettes/ggsci.html

Fun:

title: Basics of data visualization - theory and practice
description:
[Laura Garrison](https://www.laura-garrison.com/) (PhD in Data Visualization from UiB) will teach us a bit of theory behind creating stunning visualizations, while Julia Romanowska will show us how to implement this in {ggplot2}.

If you want to participate in the hands-on part, be sure to bring your laptop with R and RStudio installed, as well as the following R packages:

* tidyverse
* here
* patchwork
* ggiraph

The workshop materials will be made available via GitHub before the session.

**Brief About Laura:**
I use visualization to ask questions and tell stories about data. Earning my PhD in Visualization from the University of Bergen in 2022, my work focuses on visual exploration and communication of information, mainly from the health and life sciences. I am also a professional biomedical artist, meaning I've drawn guts for a living. Prior to my PhD, I worked for six years as an artist and content director in medical education start-ups in Silicon Valley, Chicago, and New York City. I love combining art, science, and technology to help people understand data or concepts, especially in the areas of biology and medicine. More recently, I've become interested in developing visualizations that are more accessible, i.e., usable and understandable, for different segments of the population. Currently, I divide my time as a consultant at Bouvet ASA and at the Mohn Medical Imaging and Visualization Centre (MMIV) at Haukeland Univ. Hospital in Bergen.

title: Cluster analysis in R with Cait Robinson
description:
This talk and workshop will introduce you to k-means cluster analysis using RStudio. K-means clustering groups similar items in the form of clusters. It is a useful approach for identifying patterns in a multi-dimensional dataset. During the session we will run through the fundamentals of the technique in an accessible way. We will also consider how best to visualise the outputs of our clustering. The session will focus on the example of energy inequalities in Greater London, but the technique has a wide range of application areas across different disciplines and sectors. After an introduction to the technique, you will have time to have a go of the clustering yourself. All of the code, and detailed walk throughs, will be provided.

\*\*Please bring a laptop so you can fully participate in the workshop.\*\*

After the workshop, we will have ample time for networking and chat over some snacks and drinks (courtesy of the Software Sustainability Institute).

*Cait is a quantitative human geographer, her research investigates the causes and consequences of different types of spatial inequality, with a particular interest in energy, climate and urban inequalites. She uses spatial, quantitative datasets and methods to understand inequality across multiple scales.*
*​*
*In 2022 she joined the University of Bristol as an Academic Fellow and Proleptic Lecturer in the School of Geographical Sciences. She is currently a [UKRI Future Leaders Fellow](https://news.liverpool.ac.uk/2021/09/08/five-liverpool-researchers-awarded-future-leaders-fellowships/), working on a four year project mapping ambient vulnerabilities in cities.*

title: Meaningful data visualisations in R ggplot2
description:
Welcome to the first R-Ladies Wellington meetup event!

In this event, we will be focusing on the R package ggplot2 for data visualisation. The workshop will cover the basics of the package and how to use it to create meaningful and informative graphics that effectively communicate your data's message.

In addition to the workshop, we will have some time set aside for networking, where you can connect with other R coders and build new connections within the community. We encourage attendees to bring along their laptops and any datasets they would like to work with during the workshop.

We would like to express our gratitude to Nicholson Consulting for generously offering their space to support the establishment of the R-Ladies community in Wellington.

We look forward to seeing you there!

Please note: this event has a capacity limit, and we may not be able to accommodate additional attendees beyond that limit. However, we will be sharing a video recording and slides of the workshop after the event for those who are unable to attend in person.

title: Debugging in R with Shannon Pileggi
description:
**Save the date - Event coming up soon!**
(We are on a roll this year)

Dear R-Ladies SB,
We are excited to announce our upcoming event with the extraordinary [Shannon Pileggi](https://www.pipinghotdata.com/) on ***Debugging***!

Learn how to unlock your programming superpower with debugging techniques! In this workshop, we will review code troubleshooting tips, discuss debugging functions (*traceback()*, *browser()*, *debug()*, *trace()*, and *recover()*), and distinguish between strategies for debugging your own code versus someone else’s code.

**Event Details**
**When:** Wednesday, April 12, 2023, 5 to 7pm PDT
**Where:** Remote (We will provide a link closer to the day of the event)
**Details:** This will be a hands-on workshop in Posit (RStudio) Cloud. This is a special instance of RStudio that you access through your browser. We will share details on how to log in to Posit Cloud when we send out the Zoom link closer to the event.
**Agenda:** We will open the zoom room at 5pm PST to say hi, mingle and make sure everyone has access to Posit Cloud. The workshop will start at 5:15pm PDT.

**Speaker:** Shannon is an enthusiastic professional educator, data scientist, and statistical consultant. As an R-Ladies Global leadership team member, she advocates for gender diversity in the R community and supports local R-Ladies chapters. She works as a Data Scientist at The Prostate Cancer Clinical Trials Consortium. In the past, Shannon has contributed to research on parasitic diseases at the CDC, developed curriculum at the Institute for Quantitative Theory and Methods at Emory University, and instructed advanced programming and modeling courses at California Polytechnic State University, San Luis Obispo.

title: the Basics of Visualising Map Data in R using ggplot2
description:
**SPEAKER**
Dana Roemling (she/they) is a Ph.D. student in Applied Linguistics at the University of Birmingham. Their research interests are in forensic linguistics, language and law, sociolinguistics, and lavender linguistics. Their PhD focuses on dialect features in authorship profiling. Although they worked in IT before doing their PhD, they only really started programming during their PhD studies. Now they have grown to enjoy R.
You can find Dana on her personal web or on Twitter
https://danaroemling.com/
@danaroemling

**Topic**
This workshop is designed to provide you with the basics of visualising map data in R using ggplot2. The workshop will provide a brief introduction into mapping data, which is useful, for example, in regional dialectology or typology - or generally when your data is regionally distributed. We’ll focus on creating maps from scratch and mapping linguistic variables using R so that we can visualise and identify patterns in the data.

**PREPARATION**
The R packages we'll need are: maps, rworldmap, sf, tidyverse, dplyr
They can sometimes be tricky to install, so I suggest you try it beforehand.
The data and code we'll work with will be available here: [https://github.com/danaroemling/mapping](https://github.com/danaroemling/mapping)

**ONLINE PROGRAMME**
The workshop will be held online, starting on 5th April at 16:00 (UK time). Please join us via this Zoom link:
[https://bham-ac-uk.zoom.us/j/89852185657?pwd=VWRXRFhkWTVqK0FjOG1ZVm83VzhXUT09](https://bham-ac-uk.zoom.us/j/89852185657?pwd=VWRXRFhkWTVqK0FjOG1ZVm83VzhXUT09)

CODE OF CONDUCT
Please take a minute to read our code of conduct - we are committed to creating a safe and harassment-free environment:
https://github.com/rladies/.github/blob/master/CODE_OF_CONDUCT.md
R-Ladies events are always free and open to anyone who wants to participate as long as they follow the code of conduct. We are pro-actively inclusive of queer, trans, and all minority identities, with additional sensitivity to intersectional identities. Our priority is to provide a safe community space for anyone identifying as a minority gender who is interested in and/or working with R.

title: Tidy data: Wrangling for statistical analysis in R
description:
We're starting a brand new series walking you through all the steps necessary to prepare, explore, and analyse your data in R!

This first workshop covers the concepts of "tidy data" and data wrangling commands from the tidyverse packages: the essentials for getting your data into the right format for visualisation and model fitting.

This event is suitable for beginners - you should know how to load packages and read data into R. Feel free to take a look at our video that covers these tasks: https://youtu.be/NO6ikdBxSwg

\-\-\-

It's important to us to ensure that R-Ladies Freiburg events are a safe space for anyone of a minority gender, specifically including transgender, gender non-conforming and nonbinary people as well as folks questioning their gender. All are welcome at this meetup but we specifically ask cisgender men to take a passive role and hold comments / questions until after others have had the chance to ask. Thank you for your support!

title: Creating your Data Science Portfolio
description:
Creating a data science portfolio can help you to develop your skills, and well as showcase them to the wider world. However, getting started on making your portfolio can feel like a huge task. In this talk, I'll discuss how I used #TidyTuesday to gradually build an R portfolio.

We'll run through an example of a #TidyTuesday contribution, including:

* downloading data
* initial data exploration
* creating and styling your plots
* sharing your data visualisations and code

.....and just like that, you have a portfolio!

title: BlgrdR: Make you code more shareable, reusable and reproducible with R packages
description:
Dear R ladies, BelgradeR colleagues are organizing online Meetup on Tuesday.

Details and link:

**Wrap it up: Make you code more shareable, reusable and reproducible with R packages**
**Speaker: Mike Page**
**N.B.** This is an **online event**.
**Abstract**
R packages are the fundamental unit of reproducible R code. In this talk, Mike is going to make a case that everybody writing R code should be thinking about abstracting their work into this format, from stand-alone analyses to Shiny apps. The talk will be grounded in real-world examples from data science work performed at the British Red Cross, discussing the benefits and costs of approaching everything as an R package. The talk will conclude with some concrete advice on how to improve your workflow using an R package structure.
**Bio**
Mike Page is a data scientist on the Strategic Insight and Foresight team at the British Red Cross. Here, he helps to develop a suite of open source tools, such as the Covid-19 Vulnerability Index, to help operational decision making in the charity and the broader voluntary and community sector. Mike is also the author of several R packages, including geographr and mortyr. In his spare time you can find him mountain biking and rock climbing around the Alps.
**More:**

* [https://www.mikejohnpage.com/](https://www.mikejohnpage.com/)
* [https://github.com/humaniverse](https://github.com/humaniverse)

title: Boook Club "Machine Learning with R" - Clustering
description:
Join us for our new online boookclub session discussing ‘Hands-On Machine Learning with R’ by Bradley Boehmke and Brandon Greenwell!

Monday March 27, we will talk about pt IV of the book: Clustering! From the book:

In PART III of this book we focused on methods for reducing the dimension of our feature space (p). The remaining chapters concern methods for reducing the dimension of our observation space (n); these methods are commonly referred to as *clustering*. *K*-means clustering is one of the most commonly used clustering algorithms for partitioning observations into a set of k groups (i.e. k clusters), where k is pre-specified by the analyst. *k*-means, like other clustering algorithms, tries to classify observations into mutually exclusive groups (or clusters), such that observations within the same cluster are as similar as possible (i.e., high intra-class similarity), whereas observations from different clusters are as dissimilar as possible (i.e., low inter-class similarity). In *k*-means clustering, each cluster is represented by its center (i.e, centroid) which corresponds to the mean of the observation values assigned to the cluster. The procedure used to find these clusters is similar to the *k*-nearest neighbor (KNN) algorithm discussed in Chapter [8](https://bradleyboehmke.github.io/HOML/knn.html#knn); albeit, without the need to predict an average response value.

These chapters (20-22) will be presented by one of the organizers of R Ladies Den Bosch: Martine.

The attentive reader might notice that we skipped part III of the book: dimension reduction. This part contains chapter 17 Principal Components Analysis, 18 Generalised Low Rank models, and 19 Autoencoders. If you are looking for a fun little challenge and would like to present one or more of these chapters, please send us a message!

title: movedesign app: como planear um estudo de movimento animal
description:
```
Neste evento, iremos conhecer o movedesign app, um shiny app desenvolvido por Inês Silva e seus colaboradores e entender um pouco sobre as análises de movimento animal que a pesquisadora tem utilizado. Inês também irá explicar como criar uma aplicação destas no R, com um pequeno exemplo prático de como podemos começar.

O movedesign é um shiny app amigável que auxilia os pesquisadores no delineamento de projetos relacionados com a ecologia do movimento, especificamente a análise de dados como a estimativa da área de vida, a velocidade e a distância percorrida.

Para mais informações sobre o movedesign, consulte o repositório do app https://github.com/ecoisilva/movedesign ou a preprint https://www.biorxiv.org/content/10.1101/2023.01.27.525894v1

Link do evento: O link do evento será disponibilizado após a inscrição. Atenção, você receberá uma senha de acesso pelo email. Verifique a sua caixa de Spam. Você também pode nos contactar pela seção "Comentários" aqui na página do evento ou escrever para ribeirao-preto@rladies.org.

A nossa missão é promover o uso da linguagem R entre grupos minorizados incluindo mulheres cis, mulheres trans, pessoas não-binárias, entre outros grupos subrepresentados para uma comunidade mais diversa e inclusiva.

title: Introducción a Tidymodels. Parte 1: modelos lineales
description:
¡Hola!

¡Volvimos!

Si tienes un manejo básico de R y tidyverse, y quieres comenzar a generar modelos predictivos de regresión o clasificación, este taller es para ti. Veremos las funciones principales del paquete tidymodels y desarrollaremos un modelo lineal de regresión. Esta sesión estará a cargo de Sara Acevedo, Doctora en Ciencias de la Ingeniería.

REQUERIMIENTOS

Antes del taller es necesario que instales los paquetes {tidyverse} y {tidymodels}, junto con sus dependencias:
\> install\.packages\(c\("tidyverse"\, "tidymodels"\)\, dependencies = TRUE\)
Asimismo, es necesario tener los paquetes {remotes} y {ggsignif}, junto con sus dependencias:
\> install\.packages\(c\("remotes"\, "ggsignif"\)\, dependencies = TRUE\)
Además, debes instalar desde GitHub los paquete {datos} y {corrr} (ojo que tienes que tener instalado el paquete {remotes} para que funcionen las siguientes líneas de código):
\> remotes::install_github\("cienciadedatos/datos"\)
\> remotes::install_github\("tidymodels/corrr"\)

CÓDIGO DE CONDUCTA
Queremos que este sea un espacio de aprendizaje amable y seguro. Al inscribirte en este evento estás aceptando nuestro código de conducta: https://guide.rladies.org/about/coc/#spanish

¡Te esperamos!

title: Women in Community International Women’s Day
description:
Join us for a remarkable event, "Women in Community International Women's Day" and celebrate the tremendous achievements of women around the world! On March 25, 2023, we are gathering an exceptional group of inspiring women, who have made an outstanding contribution to their respective fields within the community. This event is all about celebrating diversity, inclusion, and women's empowerment.
Our panelists will share their stories of breaking down barriers and fighting for equality and justice. Our audience can expect to hear stories of bravery, resilience, and strength, and learn about the challenges women face in various aspects of community building.
This event is perfect for anyone who is passionate about women's rights, gender equality, and making the world a better place for all. Expect to leave feeling inspired, empowered, and equipped with the tools and knowledge to drive change in your own community.
Don't miss this fantastic opportunity to celebrate International Women's Day with some of the most incredible women you'll ever meet.

For questions, send an email to lagos@rladies.org

title: Introduction to NLP with R
description:
This light introduction to working with unstructured text data while leveraging Natural Language Processing (NLP) techniques will provide a glance of the underlying approach, cover tips on how to get started and structure your project and show examples on how to transform text into usable insights.
This presentation is suitable for novice to intermediate practitioners who want to learn more about this topic. No prior knowledge is required, and anyone interested is encouraged to attend.
Use passcode 907450 to join the meeting

title: Intro to data analysis and visualization with R
description:
This workshop is free and open to all. It is for beginners with no previous experience and will be taught in two sessions from 5:00 - 7:00 pm on Thursdays in Room 206 of Edmon Low Library. See the QR code above to register.

March 23rd - Basic coding and graphing
March 30th - Basic analysis

Please share with students, especially undergraduates.

title: Custom colour scales for {ggplot2}
description:
For the second dataviz lunchtime meetup, Nicola Rennie will talk about colours.

Abstract:
There are many colour palette packages in R that are compatible with {ggplot2}, but sometimes you might want to use your own choice of colours. For example, you might want them to match company or university branding guidelines. In this talk, I'll show you how to make your own custom colour scale functions to add your own colours to {ggplot2} graphics – no more copying and pasting \`scale_colour_manual()\`!

Agenda:
12 pm GMT Talk
12.20 pm GMT Q&A with the speaker
12.45 pm GMT End of the meetup

Please be aware that the talk will be at 12 pm UK time. Check your time zone before registering.

title: [ONLINE] R-Ladies RTP x SB meetup: #tidytuesday Work Group (on a Wednesday)
description:
Come join your fellow R users in R-Ladies RTP and R-Ladies Santa Barbara in a joint meet up exploring a #tidytuesday data set! For those craving human connection and some practice flexing your data science skills, this is the event for you! We will hold our event via Zoom, where you can meet other R-ladies, improve your R skills, and build your data science portfolio. This event is for people at all levels of R (beginner through advanced).

All events are intended for minoritized genders as leaders, mentors, members, and attendees. We are emphatically queer and trans friendly. Men are welcomed to attend as guests and must be accompanied by the member who invited them. This group was created so that women, non-binary, gender non-conforming, and minoritized folks have a comfortable place to learn. If you would like to attend and feel excluded, please introduce yourself to an organizer.

At this event, we'll check out the LTERdataSampler R package. Specifically, this R package contains a number of data sets from Long-term Ecological Research (LTER) sites. You can read more about it here:[ ](https://github.com/rfordatascience/tidytuesday/tree/master/data/2022/2022-06-07)[https://lter.github.io/lterdatasampler/index.html](https://lter.github.io/lterdatasampler/index.html).

**Agenda (all times in ET):**
6:30 - 6:45 pm Meet & Greet
6:45 - 7:45 pm #tidytuesday breakout rooms
7:45 - 8:00 pm Discussion

**What is #tidytuesday?**
#TidyTuesday is a weekly social data project created by the R4DS online learning community that is focused on using tidyverse packages. A new data set and accompanying article featuring an interesting plot is released on Github every Monday. The goal is to generate a copy of the original plot or create your own original approach! For more information on #tidytuesday, check out [the main repository page](https://github.com/rfordatascience/tidytuesday).

**Who is this for?**
New and experienced R users alike! Let's learn together! All are welcome, including R-Ladies RTP members and R-Ladies from around the world. This meetup is organized in collaboration with R-Ladies Santa Barbara.

**What should I bring?**
You'll need a computer with videoconferencing capabilities and R. Serve yourself a beverage and a snack to fuel your evening!

**Where will it take place?**
Please join us online via Zoom. A link to join will be provided when you RSVP.

title: What's new in the tidyverse
description:
If you're an R user, chances are you've heard of the tidyverse - a suite of packages that's revolutionized data wrangling, visualization, and analysis. And if you've been following the [tidyverse blog](https://www.tidyverse.org/blog/), you'll know there's been a veritable flood of new functions and feature releases lately.

In this talk, we'll give you the inside scoop on all the latest and greatest updates to the tidyverse. From a new family of string-separating functions to a bunch of new joins, we'll show you how this cutting-edge universe of packages can make your life as a data scientist easier, more efficient, and more intuitive. So buckle up and get ready to explore what's new in the tidyverse!

\-\-\-
Se sei un utente R, è probabile che tu abbia sentito parlare di tidyverse, un insieme di pacchetti che ha rivoluzionato la gestione, la visualizzazione e l'analisi dei dati. E se hai seguito il blog tidyverse, saprai che ultimamente c'è stata una vera e propria ondata di nuove funzioni e nuove funzionalità.

In questo talk, scorirai in anteprima su tutti gli ultimi e i migliori aggiornamenti del pacchetto tidyverse. Da una nuova famiglia di funzioni di separazione delle stringhe a una serie di nuovi join, ti mostreremo come questo universo di pacchetti all'avanguardia può rendere la tua vita da data scientist più semplice, più efficiente e più intuitiva. Quindi allaccia le cinture e preparati a esplorare le novità in tidyverse!
\-\-\-

title: Data Science Job Search and Career Panel (RSVP in Event Description)
description:
On the hunt for a new job? Given the current labor market, R-Ladies is hosting a panel featuring recruiters and data science team leaders to provide unique insight in to the job application and interview process.

**\*\*\*IMPORTANT NOTE ABOUT RSVPs\*\*\***
Please RSVP using the following [google form](https://forms.gle/74FjvxnTBqJJi42aA) so we can reach out to you about zoom link details closer to the event date.

\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-
Event Timeline
\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-

* **7:00pm - 7:10pm: event introduction and panelist introductions**
* **7:10pm - 7:45pm: 35 minutes of prepared questions**
* **7:45pm - 8:00pm 15 minutes of audience questions**

\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-
Speakers
\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-
***Sherry Shenker*** *is a data science manager at Flatiron Health, where she helps build products to generate insights from data on real-world cancer patients. She previously worked as an applied data scientist at Civis Analytics, and graduated with a B.A. in Economics from the University of Chicago. She loves applying data science skills in new problem areas, spending as much time as possible outside, and cooking for friends and family.*

***Jaime Engle*** *started her career doing full desk tech recruiting at two different staffing agencies before going in house to* *Uptake. She was the 30th employee at Uptake and the company grew to over 850 employees and reached a $1B unicorn valuation during her three-year tenure. Then she joined a data science consulting firm- Civis Analytics for 3.5 years where she led the talent function. After that, she spent a few months at Salesforce, and then returned to the startup world at Balto, an* *AI call assist SaaS company. In January of 2023, Jaime joined Lantern Partners- a 20 year old executive search firm where she recruits C-level executives for growth stage tech companies*.

***Lauren Vecchio*** *is a holistic Talent Acquisition and Development practitioner, Lauren is currently a Learning & Development Program Implementation Manager at marketing tech startup, Wunderkind. She acts as a catalyst for employees to develop professionally, spring boarding their careers and meeting their fullest potential. Prior to that, Lauren held several talent acquisition roles where she bridged the gap between top tech talent and high-growth companies across the martech, ecommerce, fintech, and advertising industries. In her free time, you can catch her snapping photos, nerding out about personality typing, or drinking a grenadine-filled dirty shirley.*
\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-
R-Ladies is dedicated to providing a harassment-free experience for everyone. We do not tolerate harassment of participants in any form. Please take a moment to review the R-Ladies Global code of conduct (particularly if this is your first time attending!): [https://rladies.org/code-of-conduct](https://rladies.org/code-of-conduct)
For questions regarding accessibility, please email us at nyc@rladies.org.

title: Reproducible Reports in RMarkdown
description: Join us as we welcome Alex Knitter who will teach all the great ways to create reproducible reports in R.

title: Exploratory visualization of spatial data  with {mapview}
description:
Agenda:
12 pm GMT Talk
12:20 pm GMT Q&A
12.40 pm GMT Conclusion

Description
R has several great packages for creating maps, but if you're looking for speed and ease, it's hard to beat mapview. Sharon will demo how little code you need to create an interactive map to visualize, explore, and share spatial data.

!!!! Please be aware that the talk will be at 12 pm UK time. Check your time zone before registering!!!

title: Meetup R-Ladies São Paulo - Análise de dados abertos com R - Open Data Day
description:
Neste evento, o objetivo é analisar dados abertos usando R, onde as pessoas participantes irão explorar as bases de dados trabalhando juntas em pequenos grupos, com a ajuda das pessoas que realizarão monitoria sobre a base de dados, e também monitoras para as dúvidas.

Teremos pessoas mentoras para algumas bases de dados de diferentes temas (como saúde, meio ambiente, violência, etc), e várias pessoas monitoras para auxiliar.

Trata-se de de uma atividade prática de um dia inteiro, gratuita e que não exige conhecimento prévio de R ou outra linguagem de programação.

**Público-alvo: pessoas que se identificam como minoria de gênero (mulher trans ou cis, homem trans, pessoa não-binária, queer).**

Nesta edição contaremos com um espaço para incentivar a presença de mães em nossos eventos: o **GuGuDaDados!** É um espaço colaborativo e experimental que as RLadies propõem com o objetivo de deixar as crianças entretidas e cuidadas enquanto as pessoas responsáveis podem se dedicar com tranquilidade à sua formação. Saiba mais sobre o espaço GuGuDaDados neste post: [https://rladies-sp.org/posts/2023-01-evento-oficina-intro-R/#espa%C3%A7o-gugudadados](https://rladies-sp.org/posts/2023-01-evento-oficina-intro-R/#espa%C3%A7o-gugudadados)

Por favor leia todas as informações abaixo!

• **O QUE PRECISO FAZER PARA PARTICIPAR?**

* Passo 1: faça aqui sua pré inscrição no meetup - você ficará na lista de espera e tudo bem, é isso mesmo!
* Passo 2: você deverá preencher este fomulário: (que também enviaremos por email, então, nos dê seu email ;-)
* **[https://forms.gle/h1cGHioPhm9tvBFG8](https://forms.gle/h1cGHioPhm9tvBFG8)**
* Passo 3: esperar! Quando sua inscrição for realizada, você entrará na lista de presença do meetup.
* Passo 4: Você receberá um email com o status da sua inscrição - cheque seu email com frequência!

**Por que estamos fazendo isso?**
*Temos vagas reservadas para pessoas negras e indígenas, vagas reservadas para mães e vagas prioritárias para minorias de gênero.*
*E como nesta plataforma (MeetUp) não conseguimos captar essas informações, estamos experimentando este processo para garantir maior diversidade do nosso público alvo.*

• **O QUE PRECISO FAZER PARA PARTICIPAR?**

* Seguir o passo-a-passo acima
* Ler o código de conduta (mais detalhes abaixo)
* Caso seja possível, levar seu próprio notebook - não é obrigatório (e, se der, sua própria caneca!)
* **Instalar em seu notebook os programas R e RStudio ANTES do curso**: [https://www.curso-r.com/material/instalacao/](https://www.curso-r.com/material/instalacao/)
(Se não conseguir, não se preocupe que te ajudamos no dia!)
* Vontade de aprender R em um ambiente inclusivo, amistoso e seguro :)

• CÓDIGO DE CONDUTA (disponível aqui: [https://guide.rladies.org/about/coc/#portuguese](https://guide.rladies.org/about/coc/#portuguese))
Qualquer pessoa que violar ou houver previamente violado o código de conduta desta comunidade terá sua entrada proibida, será expulsa/o e/ou estará sujeita/o à ação que a pessoa organizadora achar mais apropriada, independente de inscrição no evento, ser membro do MeetUp ou qualquer outra condição.

• Temas e Monitorias

| Tema | Monitoria base de dados |
| ---- | ----------------------- |
| Violência | Fernanda Peres |
| Emprego | Ana Paula Rocha |
| Dados eleitorais | Cecília do Lago |
| Dados ambientais - Queimadas (BDQueimadas/Inpe) | Bianca Muniz

|
| Dados prisionais | Thandara Santos |
| Dados educacionais | Ana Carolina Moreno |

• CAFÉ COLABORATIVO:

* Iremos fazer um café colaborativo: quem puder levar alguma coisa para comer ou beber durante o dia, pode levar!
* Se possível traga sua caneca :D

***

• APOIO:

* Insper (cessão do espaço)
* Open Knowledge Brasil (mini-bolsa)

***

• SOBRE AS R-LADIES
R-Ladies é uma organização que promove a diversidade de gênero na comunidade da linguagem R. Nosso principal objetivo é promover a linguagem computacional estatística R compartilhando conhecimento, assim, quem tiver interesse na linguagem será bem-vinda, independente do nível de conhecimento. Nosso principal público-alvo são as pessoas que se identificam com o gênero feminino, portanto, mulheres cis, mulheres trans, bem como pessoas não-binárias e queer.

***

**O evento é gratuito, mas as vagas são limitadas. Seja consciente e só se inscreva se puder comparecer ao evento. Caso se inscreva e tenha alguma intercorrência que te impeça de ir ao evento basta nos avisar com até 1 dia de antecedência e cancelar sua inscrição na plataforma meetup :)**

title: an introduction to Bayesian statistics in R.
description:
**SPEAKER**
**Due to the train strike, today's talk is delayed to 4:45 pm. we are sorry!!!**
Dr. Mahmoud Elsherif will lead this online workshop.
Currently, Mahmoud is a researcher associate in Psychology at the University of Birmingham. He received the Leverhulme early career grant and will move to the University of Leicester to investigate linguistic predictions in younger and older adults.
Mahmoud has been using R since his Ph.D. for data analysis, modeling, and visualization.
To follow him online:
Twitter: https://twitter.com/MamsMandrill
He has many exciting and high-quality publications:
Researchgate: https://www.researchgate.net/profile/Mahmoud-Elsherif-2

**WORKSHOP OVERVIEW**
Frequentists dominated statistical practice for many years. However,
bayesian data analysis is increasingly becoming the tool of choice for many data-analysis problems. Mahmoud will present an introduction to Bayesian statistics in R.

**PREPARATION AND RESOURCES**
No, at this moment.

**ONLINE PROGRAMME**
The event will be held online and will start at 16:45 (GMT), 16th March 2023
Please join us via this Zoom link:
[https://bham-ac-uk.zoom.us/j/87167989030?pwd=WmpzQmw3UmwxTUhISkJlNmpRWWFwQT09](https://bham-ac-uk.zoom.us/j/87167989030?pwd=WmpzQmw3UmwxTUhISkJlNmpRWWFwQT09)

**CODE OF CONDUCT**
Please take a minute to read our code of conduct - we are committed to creating a safe and harassment-free environment:
https://github.com/rladies/.github/blob/master/CODE_OF_CONDUCT.md

R-Ladies events are always free and open to anyone who wants to participate as long as they follow the code of conduct. We are pro-actively inclusive of queer, trans, and all minority identities, with additional sensitivity to intersectional identities. Our priority is to provide a safe community space for anyone identifying as a minority gender who is interested in and/or working with R.

title: R-Ladies Boulder #27: Integrating R & Google Workspaces w/ Jade Yonehiro
description:
"As data analysts, we often collaborate with colleagues on analyses and data presentations who do not have R or other statistical programming skill sets. These collaborations can be frustrating for both the analyst and non-analyst, with the analyst needing to make and remake tables or visuals and the non-analyst lacking the autonomy to tweak or explore data themselves. One open access and open source based solution is to develop workflows between R and more accessible programs such as Excel or Google Sheets. The googlesheets4 package provides analysts a linking tool so they can leverage the power of R for data processing and visualization while empowering non-analysts with a user-friendly environment for collaboration."

Zoom link for remote attendees: https://us02web.zoom.us/j/85471235979?pwd=a3FBRWpubysxcDYzS1FvZlhEWVlHZz09

title: An Introduction to Spark with R
description:
With information growing at exponential rates, it’s no surprise that historians are referring to this period of history as the Information Age. The increasing speed at which data is being collected has created new opportunities and is certainly poised to create even more.
Apache Spark been a leading tool that is democratizing our ability to process large datasets, there comes `sparklyr`, a project merging R and Spark into a powerful tool that is easily accessible to all R users.
During this webinar, Edgar would take us through:

* basic understanding of Apache Spark,
* installing the sparklyr package in rstudio,
* reading data with sparklyr package, and finally
* a simple example of modeling your data

We hope to have you join us.

title: Efficient dependency management with {renv}
description:
The [{renv} package](https://rstudio.github.io/renv/articles/renv.html) can help us keep track of all the packages we're using in a specific project and create backups or freeze package versions for later use. This makes sharing and re-running code easier. Let's dive into how we can incorporate this into our projects!

This is the first meeting in a series about {renv} - [Julia Romanowska](https://www.uib.no/en/persons/Julia.Romanowska) will give us a quick intro into the package, with some examples. Then, we can discuss, mingle, and set up dates for the next meetings.

**NOTE:** the room is booked for 2 hours, but you can come and leave whenever you want.

The series is co-organized by Türküler Özgümüs and Jessica Louise Ray.

title: Dealing with spatial data using R with Alessia Calafiore
description:
**R-Ladies Edinburgh are back with our first in-person event of 2023!**

In this hands-on workshop we are going to play with some spatial data to learn about basic spatial operations and map making with R. To do this, we will leverage three great R packages, {sf}, {ggplot2} and {leaflet}. By the end of the workshop, you will be able to load, manipulate and visualise spatial data with beautiful maps. This will be an introductory workshop and no previous knowledge of spatial analysis or mapping is required.

\*\*Please bring a laptop so you can fully participate in the workshop.\*\*

After the workshop, we will have ample time for networking and chat over some snacks and drinks (courtesy of the Software Sustainability Institute).

*Alessia is a Lecturer in Sustainability and Urban Data Science at the Edinburgh Future Institute and the Edinburgh School of Architecture and Landscape Architecture of the University of Edinburgh. Prior to this, she was a postdoctoral research associate at the Geographic Data Science Lab of the University of Liverpool.*
*Alessia's research sits at the intersection of Urban Planning, Geography and Computer Science and she interested in developing new spatially informed computational methods to better understand the mutual relationship between human behaviours and their urban contexts. Her current substantive focus concerns how we can equitability manage Net Zero transitions within cities.*

title: Book Club "Machine Learning with R" - Interpretable Machine Learning
description:
Join us for our new online boookclub session discussing ‘Hands-On Machine Learning with R’ by Bradley Boehmke and Brandon Greenwell!

Monday March 6, we will talk about interpretable machine learning
From the book:

In the previous chapters you learned how to train several different forms of advanced ML models. Often, these models are considered “black boxes” due to their complex inner-workings. However, because of their complexity, they are typically more accurate for predicting nonlinear, faint, or rare phenomena. Unfortunately, more accuracy often comes at the expense of interpretability, and interpretability is crucial for business adoption, model documentation, regulatory oversight, and human acceptance and trust. Luckily, several advancements have been made to aid in interpreting ML models over the years and this chapter demonstrates how you can use them to extract important insights.
This chapter will be presented by one of the authors of the book: Brandon Greenwell!

title: ChatGPT 與他們的產地
description:
2023 年即將是 AI 大爆發的一年，不但各種超強人工智慧會橫空出世，更多衍生出來的軟體應用，可能會無孔不入地入侵你的生活！
為了讓大家無痛接軌跟上這班車，本活動邀請了微軟的專家們，用3小時的時間，讓你追上這個 AI 畫圖和對話機器人的時代，快速站上 AI 浪潮！

本活動適合各種程度的群眾參加，不論你是否懂 AI，是否會寫程式，只要你有興趣了解最新的 AI 議題，都歡迎你來與我們分享及交流！

P.S.:

* 終於要辦實體活動啦～～
* 感謝各單位的贊助與幫忙，我們將提供各式小贈品，等著大家來領取，先到先拿，送完為止。
* 現場也會備有一些小點心，歡迎大家跟我們一邊吃吃喝喝，一邊跟大家交流。

**[注意事項]**

*R-Ladies Taipei 是女性為主的社群，不過，為了慶祝久違的實體活動，此次活動特地開放男性參加。但是與此同時，我們還是希望參加的男性受眾盡量與女性朋友協同參加。*

**[活動場地]**

*digiBlock C 數位創新基地*
臺北市大同區承德路三段287號
距離圓山捷運站2號出口約5分鐘

**[活動網站]**

[https://globalai.community/bootcamp-2023/asia-r-ladies-taipei-6035/](https://globalai.community/bootcamp-2023/asia-r-ladies-taipei-6035/)

**[議程]**
***人數眾多，請各位儘早報到***
13:30 \~ 14:15 報到
14:15 \~ 14:30 微軟？OpenAI？ChatGPT？各種 AI 產品大爆發 - Ning Chen
14:30 \~ 15:15 Global AI Bootcamp 2023 Keynote - Kui-Ming Chen
15:15 \~ 16:00 Form Recognizer: 不寫程式也能從零打造自己的 AI 紙本表單辨識系統 - Kristen Chan

16:30 \~ 16:45 AI 生成海報的利器：Designer - Kui-Ming Chen
16:45 \~ 17:00 把 ChatGPT 收到自己的 iphone 裡 - Kui-Ming Chen
17:00 \~ 17:15 Azure OpenAI 使用案例分享 - Kristen Chan
17:15 \~ 17:30 自由交流時間

**[講者與主持簡介]**

*Kristen Chan*
台灣微軟 Microsoft Technical Trainer / R-Ladies Taipei 現任負責人
喜歡學任何新鮮有趣的東西，期望能貢獻自己的能力運用資料解決問題。

*Kui-Ming Chen (Benjamin)*
Chimes AI 技術顧問 / Microsoft MVP
致力於用資料科學解決各個領域的疑難雜症，舉凡股票、期貨、廣告、生技醫療和環保，都有實務處理的經驗。除了資料科學以外，近年也投入參與 IoT 的開發與專利申請，開始進入跨足 AI、IoT、雲端的三棲狀態。

*Gene Liang*
商智雲 協理 / Microsoft MVP / 科技 Youtuber
對人工智慧和低代碼開發投入實務應用有極大熱忱，積極吸收資訊產業對新技術的發展。除此之外，我也是一個喜歡跑步和閱讀科幻小說的人，喜歡在閒暇時間裡放鬆心情。我相信，通過不斷學習和挑戰自我，我能夠不斷創造自己的價值。

*孫玉峰*
台灣角川數據架構師
在多個產業開發各種資料產品，包含推薦系統以及廣告數據平台（DMP／CDP），近年來的公開演講以及實務上則專注在資料工程與 MLOps 的建立，期望讓數據驅動（Data-Driven）的觀念帶動各個產業領域的創新。《數位時代》專欄作家、微軟最有價值專家（MVP）以及 Taiwan R User Group（常態活動 MLDM Monday 主辦單位）的共同主持人（Co-organizer）。

*Ning Chen*
Microsoft MVP / R-Ladies Taipei Organizer / D4SG Manager
參與推動data for social good資料英雄計畫 [http://d4sg.org/fellowship/](http://d4sg.org/fellowship/)，此計劃運作六年，推動公益性質組織及公部門的大量營運數據發揮價值。歷年來，已經有超過40個政府機關及NGO透過這個計畫更加深入了解資料價值，輔導過社會及環保相關議題的資料分析案，2017\~2022連續五年在AI類別取得微軟MVP榮譽，活耀於AI、機器學習相關研究、社群、競賽活動並且多場海內外AI相關專題演講經驗。帶領AI團隊與台北市社會救助科拿到聯發科「智在家鄉」百萬首獎。

title: Tidy Tuesday
description:
❤️ **R-Ladies Helsinki is back!** ❤️

See you soon! ❤️

title: Blog Writing Workshop
description:
Do you want to start a technical blog? Curious about how to publish your blog? Do you have blog ideas but want to get feedback? Well then this is the Meetup for you!

We will work in small groups to workshop your blog ideas. Don't have any ideas yet? No problem! Here are a few topics that would make for a good blog post:

* Share your experience of learning a new programming language
* Write about your favorite packages or functions
* Talk about a recent data set or project you worked on

How do you get your blog out there?! **R-Ladies Rome is hosting a Meetup about Quarto**, which can be used to publish blogs! [Join R-Ladies Rome on Monday February 13th at 1:00 pm EST](https://www.meetup.com/rladies-rome/events/290673316/) to learn more about all the cool things Quarto can do.

Can't make it? No problem! We will share resources about Quarto during our Meetup. And R-Ladies NYC can host your blog post on our [website](https://www.rladiesnyc.org/)! Email us at nyc@rladies.org if you are interested in utilizing our blog.

This event will be hosted over Zoom; the link will be available on this page the morning of the event, and the passcode will be emailed to you. **Fill out this form ([https://forms.gle/RHgWsCo8FqnZyQgu7](https://forms.gle/RHgWsCo8FqnZyQgu7)) if you are interested in attending this Blog Writing Workshop**, to ensure you receive the Zoom information the day of the event.

R-Ladies is dedicated to providing a harassment-free experience for everyone. We do not tolerate harassment of participants in any form. Please take a moment to review the R-Ladies Global code of conduct (particularly if this is your first time attending): [https://rladies.org/code-of-conduct](https://rladies.org/code-of-conduct)

For questions regarding accessibility, please email us at nyc@rladies.org.

title: Careers in Public Health - IN PERSON REGISTRATION ON EVENTBRITE
description:
**REGISTER IN PERSON ATTENDANCE ON EVENTBRITE: https://www.eventbrite.com/e/navigating-public-health-careers-tickets-523492459037**

Hi everyone,

H﻿ave you ever thought about what a career in public health could look like and how you get there?

Our February event will showcase a panel of experts sharing the different journeys that brought them to their current roles in public health so as to share with young or/and aspiring epidemiologists/statisticians/lab scientists etc.. what it takes to build a career in this sector and what are the tasks and roles and the different types of careers that one one can build.

This event is co-hosted between R-Ladies Melbourne and Women in Bioinformatics (https://twitter.com/bioinfowomen), a group founded in 2021 by Dr. Nikeisha Caruana that aims at bringing together women and minorities within the Australia bioinformatics community.

The doors will open at 5.30pm with pizza, drinks and networking.
The presentations part of the event will start at 6pm with the speaker's career journey introductions and it will be followed by a panel discussion.

**This is a hybrid event and the zoom meeting passcode will be available on the night of the event.**
**Register your in-person attendance here: https://www.eventbrite.com/e/navigating-public-health-careers-tickets-523492459037**

The doors will open at 5.30pm with pizza, drinks and networking.
The presentations part of the event will start at 6pm with the speaker's career journey introductions and it will be followed by a panel discussion.

**Speakers**

**Steph Main (she/her)**
Steph is an applied epidemiologist and global health and development practitioner and researcher. She is currently working at the Melbourne North Eastern Public Health Unit, with a focus on communicable disease prevention, promotion and protection. She trained through Australia’s accredited Field Epidemiology Training Program at the Australian National University. Her recent work has included COVID-19 outbreak response; communicable disease surveillance and outbreak management in Melbourne; Tuberculosis research and public health programming in Papua New Guinea and Indonesia; and observational and implementation research.

**Danielle Ingle (she/her)**
Dr Danielle Ingle joined the Department of Microbiology and Immunology at the University of Melbourne in late 2020 as an NHMRC Emerging Leadership Fellow. This fellowship will integrate microbial genomics with population modelling, antimicrobial resistance data and epidemiology of enteric bacterial pathogens that represent significant threats to public health. Danielle completed her PhD at the University of Melbourne in 2017. She then moved to the Australian National University as a Research Fellow investigating the evolution and epidemiology of multidrug resistant enteric bacteria.

**Daniella Hock (she/her)**
Dr Daniella Hock is a Research Fellow in clinical proteomics at the University of Melbourne. During her career, she has worked in both industry and research in Australia and Brazil. Her current work focuses on translating proteomics into a clinical test to improve the genetic diagnosis of rare diseases as about half of the patients remain undiagnosed, some of them for years.

Looking forward to seeing you all!

R-Ladies Melbourne and Women in Bioinformatics

title: Beautifully annotated: enhancing your ggplots with text
description:
We are pleased to start this first data visualisation's series of lunch with R-Ladies.

Programme:
12 pm Talk
12:20 pm Q&A
12.40 Conclusion

Description:
Applying design principles to the text in our graphs, and making text an integral part of the visualisation, can make all the difference between a busy plot and a plot which communicates a clear story.

In this session, we will explore:
— how to be less dependent on annotations (I know… trust me!)

— how to use colours and fonts to draw attention to key elements in annotations

— how to format text on the fly to change fonts, colours, alignments, and text sizes using \`{ggtext}\`

— how to add arrows to your plots to highlight key data points or important patterns

— a few useful packages and ggplot tricks when it comes to annotations

Attendees are welcome to bring along one of their own plots to discuss during the Q&A session for some live feedback.

title: Welcome to R-Ladies Halifax! Introductory Meeting
description:
**Welcome to R-Ladies!**

We are happy to announce the first meeting of R-Ladies Halifax!

This will be a very informal first meeting where we will discuss what you can expect from the chapter. We will have some examples of what R-Ladies looks like in other areas and hopefully find format and content that fits the needs and interests of our Halifax audience.

This will be a great opportunity to connect with other local R users. Please bring any ideas and questions!

We will have a fun tidbit to share as well, how to customize your R-Studio theme to make your workspace as clean or colourful as you like!

title: Regular Expressions and Working with Text Strings
description:
Workshop description:

In R, strings are used for storing text. Most of the data that we work with contains strings, either in object or file names or as values in data frames or other tabular formats. Regular expressions are specially encoded strings of characters that match patterns in other strings. Being able to write regular expressions is an underutilized, valuable skill that can save us time and lines of code when we need to match, extract, or transform text by matching words, characters, and patterns. If a text pattern can be described verbally, we can most likely write a regular expression to match it. For example, we can use regular expressions to match dates, email addresses, strings that start or end with a particular character, etc. This talk will cover the building blocks of regular expressions and how we can use them in R to work with strings, focusing on packages built specifically for string manipulation. This talk is meant to introduce regex to those without prior knowledge and provide a boost for people already familiar.

Link will be posted closer to the event.

title: Add JavaScript data viz to your data science toolkit with Observable Plot
description:
Hi R-Ladies SB!

We are thrilled to welcome Allison Horst & Ananya Roy for our first ever ***JavaScript*** lesson using Observable Plot -- this hands-on workshop-style event may be a slight departure from our R-based meetups in the past, but fear not -- you can expect to see hints of {ggplot2}'s influence in this new-to-us tool!

Please see Allison & Ananya's event description below for more details, and note that this is a **virtual-only event** (Zoom link available upon RSVP).

Be sure to fill out [this fun survey](https://docs.google.com/forms/d/e/1FAIpQLScspHfdtwddLsVhJEEywUEIXE7CM6Zt7qsVn8kRVgdvxGZb0g/viewform?usp=send_form) (seriously, it's fun and had us laughing through it all) by Wednesday 2/15 @ 5pm PT so that we have a rad dataset to explore during the workshop!

See you there!

\~ Sam, An, Danielle, & Camila

\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-

**Description:** We're increasingly seeing different languages and tools combined in computational notebooks. For example, Quarto makes it possible to add Observable JavaScript to .qmd documents, creating exciting opportunities to include custom interactive data visualizations for the web alongside your R code and analyses. How can you get started with data visualization in JavaScript?

In this session, we'll introduce [Observable Plot](https://observablehq.com/@observablehq/plot) \- a JavaScript package with a goal of getting you to "a meaningful visualization quickly\." In particular\, we'll highlight how your grammar of graphics and ggplot2 knowledge maps closely onto Plot code\, and explore how Observable Inputs get you quickly from static to interactive web\-ready visualizations\.

**Beforehand:** We'll be working in Observable notebooks. If you want to save your work from this session, you can create a free account at [observablehq.com](http://observablehq.com). Or, you can follow along and make changes to our shared notebook without creating an account to test it out (in that case, your changes just won't get saved).

**Pre-meetup fun survey:**

We want to explore some data that feels relevant. *Can you help us out?* We've made this [fun little survey](https://forms.gle/LxFupn6wM7KBcqrH6) to build a play dataset for use during the session. Thank you for filling it out by **5pm PT on Wednesday 2/15!**

title: Boook club “Hands-On Machine Learning with R” - Chp 15 Stacked Models
description:
Join us for our new online boookclub session discussing **‘Hands-On Machine Learning with R**’ by Bradley Boehmke and Brandon Greenwell! Monday February 20, we will talk about stacked models.

From the book:
***Stacking*** involves training a new learning algorithm to combine the predictions of several base learners. First, the base learners are trained using the available training data, then a combiner or meta algorithm, called the *super learner*, is trained to make a final prediction based on the predictions of the base learners.

This chapter will be presented by one of our community members: Ece. Ece Ozler, is from Turkey. She has a graduate degree in Economics from the Koc University in Turkey and a master's in Finance from the Netherlands. She has lived in the Netherlands for almost six years now. She works for an analytics company, FI Analytics, in the banking sector. She uses R and ML to build credit scoring models; C# to build apps that use the prediction models made in R.

**This boook club:**
For this boook club we - the Den Bosch and Utrecht chapters of R Ladies - are joining forces. We will alternate hosting the meetups. In +- 10 meetings (definitely more :) ) , we will work through all the chapters in the book together. We are doing this boookclub, because we like to learn together. It is not the case presenters are the experts on the matter. To make this a group effort, the idea is that all participants take turns in presenting chapters. We are of course available to help. If you’re not comfortable presenting, just listening is also okay.
If you cannot make it this time, feel free to join in on one of the subsequent meetings.

**For who:**

* People who want to take their first steps into machine learning
* Some prior knowledge on R programming will be helpful. As the authors of the book themselves mention: “This book is not meant to be an introduction to R or to programming in general; as we assume the reader has familiarity with the R language to include defining functions, managing R objects, controlling the flow of a program, and other basic tasks.”

**What to expect:**

* Presentations summarising the key points on each topic of the book
* Putting the theory to practice by going through exercises together
* Room to discuss questions and other thoughts

**Whereabouts**:

* Meet every 2 weeks via Zoom. The password will be sent to you via e-mail before the meeting.
* We meet every second Monday, from 7 - 8.30 pm
* It is not necessary to read the chapters in advance, but feel free to do so.

R-Ladies is dedicated to providing a harassment-free experience for everyone. We do not tolerate harassment of participants in any form. See the [code of conduct](https://rladies.org/code-of-conduct/).
The book can be found at [https://bradleyboehmke.github.io/HOML/](https://bradleyboehmke.github.io/HOML/). It is also available in print. For the slides of our previous meetings, see [https://github.com/rladiesnl/book_club_handsonML](https://github.com/rladiesnl/book_club_handsonML).
